1	\chapter{The tautological quotient and the universal property of $\mathrm{Gr}(r,d)$}
2	\label{chap:Picard_GrassmannianQuot}
3	
4	\providecommand{\ShMod}{\mathrm{SheafOfModules}}
5	
6	The previous chapter constructed the Grassmannian scheme \(\mathrm{Gr}(r,d)\) over
7	\(\mathbb{Z}\) by gluing the affine charts \(U^I\)
8	(\cref{def:gr_affine_chart}) along the transition isomorphisms
9	(\cref{def:gr_transition}) satisfying the cocycle condition
10	(\cref{lem:gr_cocycle}), and proved that the structure morphism
11	\(\mathrm{Gr}(r,d) \to \Spec \mathbb{Z}\) is separated (\cref{lem:gr_separated})
12	and proper (\cref{lem:gr_proper}). This chapter adds the next layer: the
13	\emph{tautological rank-\(d\) quotient}
14	\(u : \mathcal{O}_{\mathrm{Gr}(r,d)}^{\,r} \twoheadrightarrow \mathcal{U}\) of the
15	trivial rank-\(r\) sheaf, and the \emph{universal property} that makes
16	\(\mathrm{Gr}(r,d)\) the fine moduli space of rank-\(d\) locally free quotients of
17	\(\mathcal{O}^{r}\) -- equivalently, the representing scheme of the Quot functor
18	of the trivial sheaf.
19	
20	Throughout, \(r \ge d \ge 1\) are the fixed integers of the previous chapter, and
21	for a size-\(d\) subset \(I \subseteq \{1,\dots,r\}\) we write \(R^I\) for the
22	chart ring \(\mathbb{Z}[X^I]\) of \(U^I = \Spec R^I\) and \(R^I_J\) for the
23	localisation along \(P^I_J = \det(X^I_J)\) defining the overlap
24	\(U^I_J \subseteq U^I\).
25	
26	\section{Infrastructure: locally free sheaves and module gluing}
27	\label{sec:grquot_infra}
28	
29	The constructions of this chapter -- the universal quotient sheaf
30	\(\mathcal{U}\), the tautological quotient \(u\), and the functor of points -- rest
31	on two pieces of foundational machinery for sheaves of modules. The first is the
32	predicate ``locally free of rank \(d\)'' (\cref{def:is_locally_free_of_rank}),
33	which records that a sheaf of modules is, on the members of some open cover,
34	isomorphic to the trivial bundle \(\mathcal{O}^{d}\); it is the condition cut out
35	in the definition of the Grassmannian functor. That predicate is the project-local
36	\(\mathrm{SheafOfModules.IsLocallyFreeOfRank}\) already set up in the Quot-scheme
37	chapter (\cref{def:is_locally_free_of_rank}), which this chapter reuses verbatim;
38	the only foundational machinery genuinely new to this chapter is the second piece:
39	a gluing primitive that descends a sheaf of modules (and a morphism of such
40	sheaves) from per-chart data along an open cover, given a transition cocycle
41	compatible with the cover's own gluing; it is the vehicle that assembles the
42	per-chart free sheaves \(\mathcal{O}^{d}_{U^I}\) and the per-chart quotients
43	\(u^I\) into the global \(\mathcal{U}\) and \(u\), and is set up below.
44	
45	Stating the triple-overlap multiplicativity of the descent datum, however, first
46	requires a small piece of base-change machinery for the pullback functor on sheaves
47	of modules: the three transition isomorphisms attached to a triple of charts live a
48	priori on three different overlaps, and to compare their composite they must be
49	transported to a single common overlap. The next two blocks supply exactly this
50	transport -- the pseudofunctorial comparison of iterated pullbacks
51	(\cref{def:modules_pullbackComp}) and its packaging along the triple-overlap data of a
52	scheme glue datum (\cref{lem:modules_pullback_basechange_transport}) -- after which the
53	cocycle condition can be expressed cleanly.
54	
55	\begin{definition}[Comparison of iterated pullbacks of sheaves of modules]
56	  \label{def:modules_pullbackComp}
57	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp}
58	  \mathlibok
59	  \textit{Provided by Mathlib.}
60	  Let \(a : X \to Y\) and \(b : Y \to Z\) be morphisms of schemes. For a sheaf of
61	  \(\mathcal{O}_Z\)-modules \(\mathcal{M}\), pulling back first along \(b\) and then
62	  along \(a\) agrees with pulling back along the composite \(b \circ a\): there is a
63	  natural isomorphism of sheaves of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules
64	  \[
65	    a^{*}\bigl(b^{*}\mathcal{M}\bigr)
66	      \;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\;
67	    (b \circ a)^{*}\mathcal{M},
68	  \]
69	  natural in \(\mathcal{M}\), expressing that \(\mathcal{M} \mapsto \mathcal{M}\)'s
70	  pullback is a pseudofunctor on schemes. These comparison isomorphisms satisfy the
71	  pentagon (associativity) coherence for a triple of composable morphisms. This is the
72	  pullback half of the pseudofunctoriality of sheaves of modules and is supplied by
73	  Mathlib.
74	\end{definition}
75	
76	\begin{lemma}\leanok
77	[Base-change transport of a transition isomorphism to a triple overlap]
78	  \label{lem:modules_pullback_basechange_transport}
79	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.pullbackBaseChangeTransport}
80	  \uses{def:modules_pullbackComp, def:gr_the_glue_data}
81	  Let \(D\) be a glue datum of schemes (\cref{def:gr_the_glue_data}) with charts
82	  \(U_i\), overlaps \(V_{ij}\), overlap immersions \(f_{ij} : V_{ij} \hookrightarrow
83	  U_i\), transitions \(t_{ij} : V_{ij} \xrightarrow{\sim} V_{ji}\), and triple-overlap
84	  data \(t'_{ijk}\) with its compatibility identity \(t_{\mathrm{fac}}\). For an index
85	  triple \((i,j,k)\) write
86	  \[
87	    V_{ijk} \;=\; V_{ij} \times_{U_i} V_{ik}
88	  \]
89	  for the common triple overlap, with the two projections
90	  \(p^{ij}_{ijk} : V_{ijk} \to V_{ij}\) and \(p^{ik}_{ijk} : V_{ijk} \to V_{ik}\), and
91	  let \(t'_{ijk} : V_{ijk} \xrightarrow{\sim} V_{jki}\) be the glue datum's triple
92	  transition, satisfying
93	  \[
94	    t'_{ijk} \,\circ\, p^{jk}_{jki} \;=\; p^{ij}_{ijk} \,\circ\, t_{ij}
95	    \qquad (t_{\mathrm{fac}}).
96	  \]
97	  Given per-chart sheaves of modules \(\mathcal{M}_i\) and a transition isomorphism
98	  \[
99	    g_{ij} \;:\; f_{ij}^{*}\,\mathcal{M}_i
100	      \;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\;
101	    (t_{ij} \circ f_{ji})^{*}\,\mathcal{M}_j
102	  \]
103	  on the overlap \(V_{ij}\) (as in \cref{def:scheme_modules_glue}), the
104	  \emph{base-change transport} of \(g_{ij}\) to the triple overlap \(V_{ijk}\) is the
105	  isomorphism of sheaves of \(\mathcal{O}_{V_{ijk}}\)-modules
106	  \[
107	    \widehat{g}_{ij}^{\,k}
108	      \;:\;
109	    (f_{ij} \circ p^{ij}_{ijk})^{*}\,\mathcal{M}_i
110	      \;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\;
111	    (t_{ij} \circ f_{ji} \circ p^{ij}_{ijk})^{*}\,\mathcal{M}_j
112	  \]
113	  obtained by pulling \(g_{ij}\) back along \(p^{ij}_{ijk}\) and reassociating the
114	  iterated pullbacks via \cref{def:modules_pullbackComp}. With the analogous transports
115	  \(\widehat{g}_{jk}^{\,i}\) and \(\widehat{g}_{ik}^{\,j}\) (the first taken along
116	  \(t'_{ijk}\), so that \(t_{\mathrm{fac}}\) identifies its source and target sheaves
117	  with those of \(\widehat{g}_{ij}^{\,k}\) and \(\widehat{g}_{ik}^{\,j}\)), all three
118	  transported isomorphisms become endomorphism-composable: \(\widehat{g}_{ij}^{\,k}\)
119	  and \(\widehat{g}_{jk}^{\,i}\) land on a common sheaf, and their composite is
120	  comparable to \(\widehat{g}_{ik}^{\,j}\) on \(V_{ijk}\).
121	\end{lemma}
122	
123	\begin{proof}
124	  \uses{def:modules_pullbackComp, def:gr_the_glue_data}
125	  \leanok
126	  The pullback functor on sheaves of modules is a pseudofunctor: for composable
127	  scheme morphisms it carries the comparison isomorphisms of
128	  \cref{def:modules_pullbackComp}, natural in the sheaf and coherent for triple
129	  composites. Pulling the transition isomorphism \(g_{ij}\) back along the projection
130	  \(p^{ij}_{ijk} : V_{ijk} \to V_{ij}\) yields an isomorphism between
131	  \((p^{ij}_{ijk})^{*} f_{ij}^{*}\mathcal{M}_i\) and \((p^{ij}_{ijk})^{*}(t_{ij} \circ
132	  f_{ji})^{*}\mathcal{M}_j\); applying \cref{def:modules_pullbackComp} to each side
133	  reassociates these into the single-pullback form
134	  \((f_{ij} \circ p^{ij}_{ijk})^{*}\mathcal{M}_i\) and
135	  \((t_{ij} \circ f_{ji} \circ p^{ij}_{ijk})^{*}\mathcal{M}_j\), giving
136	  \(\widehat{g}_{ij}^{\,k}\). For the \((j,k)\)-transition the same construction is
137	  applied along the triple transition \(t'_{ijk}\) rather than a bare projection; the
138	  glue datum's compatibility identity \(t_{\mathrm{fac}}\),
139	  \(t'_{ijk} \circ p^{jk}_{jki} = p^{ij}_{ijk} \circ t_{ij}\), guarantees that the
140	  source of \(\widehat{g}_{jk}^{\,i}\) coincides with the target of
141	  \(\widehat{g}_{ij}^{\,k}\) (both are the pullback of \(\mathcal{M}_j\) along the same
142	  morphism \(V_{ijk} \to U_j\)), so the two are composable as endomorphism-style
143	  isomorphisms over \(V_{ijk}\). The reassociations introduced by
144	  \cref{def:modules_pullbackComp} are coherent (they satisfy the pentagon identity), so
145	  the composite \(\widehat{g}_{jk}^{\,i} \circ \widehat{g}_{ij}^{\,k}\) and the third
146	  transport \(\widehat{g}_{ik}^{\,j}\) are isomorphisms between the same pair of
147	  sheaves on \(V_{ijk}\), and may be compared directly. This is the module-level
148	  analogue of the scheme-level triangle \(t'_{ijk}\)/\(t_{\mathrm{fac}}\) and is the
149	  shape in which the multiplicative cocycle condition is stated below.
150	\end{proof}
151	
152	\begin{lemma}[Triple-overlap endpoint bridges]
153	  \label{lem:gr_glueData_bridges}
154	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.glueData_bridge_src,
155	        AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.glueData_bridge_mid,
156	        AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.glueData_bridge_tgt}
157	  \uses{def:gr_the_glue_data}
158	  Let \(D\) be a glue datum of schemes (\cref{def:gr_the_glue_data}) and fix
159	  indices \(i,j,k\) with triple overlap \(V_{ijk} = V_{ij} \times_{U_i} V_{ik}\).
160	  The three transports of \cref{lem:modules_pullback_basechange_transport} have
161	  endpoints pinned down by three scheme-morphism equalities on \(V_{ijk}\), each a
162	  pure consequence of the glue datum's structural identities:
163	  \begin{itemize}
164	    \item (\emph{src}) \(\mathrm{fst} \circ f_{ij} = \mathrm{snd} \circ f_{ik}\)
165	      -- the defining pullback condition of \(V_{ijk}\);
166	    \item (\emph{mid}) \(\mathrm{fst} \circ (t_{ij} \circ f_{ji})
167	      = (t'_{ijk} \circ \mathrm{fst}) \circ f_{jk}\) -- the pullback condition
168	      composed with the glue datum's \(t_{\mathrm{fac}}\) identity;
169	    \item (\emph{tgt}) \((t'_{ijk} \circ \mathrm{fst}) \circ (t_{jk} \circ f_{kj})
170	      = \mathrm{snd} \circ (t_{ik} \circ f_{ki})\) -- the cocycle core, from the
171	      \(t_{\mathrm{fac}}\) associativity, the reversed pullback condition, the
172	      \(t_{\mathrm{inv}}\) collapse and the glue datum's cocycle identity.
173	  \end{itemize}
174	  These three equalities align the source, middle, and target sheaves of the three
175	  transports so that the cocycle equation (C2) of \cref{def:scheme_modules_glue}
176	  typechecks.
177	\end{lemma}
178	\begin{proof}  \uses{def:gr_the_glue_data}
179	  Each equality is an identity of morphisms in the category of schemes, proved by
180	  rewriting with the glue datum's axioms: \emph{src} is literally
181	  \(\mathrm{pullback.condition}\); \emph{mid} adds the \(t_{\mathrm{fac}}\)
182	  factorisation \(D.t_{\mathrm{fac}}\,i\,j\,k\); \emph{tgt} chains
183	  \(t_{\mathrm{fac}}\)-associativity at \((j,k,i)\), the reversed pullback
184	  condition, an auxiliary collapse combining \(t_{\mathrm{fac}}\)-associativity at
185	  \((k,i,j)\) with \(t_{\mathrm{inv}}\), and finally the cocycle identity
186	  \(D.\mathrm{cocycle}\,i\,j\,k\). No sheaf-of-modules data enters; these are
187	  bookkeeping equalities of the underlying scheme cover.
188	\end{proof}
189	
190	% HISTORICAL (abandoned route): an earlier draft built the glued sheaf as a hand-rolled
191	% presheaf of compatible families (decls gluePresheaf / gluePresheafModule / gluePresheafIsSheaf).
192	% That route is abandoned; the gluing is now the equalizer of pushforwards described in the
193	% Construction paragraph of \cref{def:scheme_modules_glue}, which subsumes it.
194	
195	\begin{definition}
196	
197	\leanok
198	[Gluing a sheaf of modules along an open cover]
199	  \label{def:scheme_modules_glue}
200	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.glue}
201	  \uses{def:gr_the_glue_data, def:modules_pullbackComp,
202	    lem:modules_pullback_basechange_transport, lem:gr_glueData_bridges}
203	  Let \(D\) be a glue datum of schemes (\cref{def:gr_the_glue_data}), with index set \(J\), charts \(U_i\),
204	  pairwise overlaps \(V_{ij}\), open immersions
205	  \(f_{ij} : V_{ij} \hookrightarrow U_i\), and transition isomorphisms
206	  \(t_{ij} : V_{ij} \xrightarrow{\sim} V_{ji}\) satisfying the glue datum's
207	  cocycle conditions; write \(X\) for the glued scheme and
208	  \(\iota_i : U_i \hookrightarrow X\) for the canonical open immersions of the
209	  charts. Suppose given:
210	  \begin{itemize}
211	    \item for each \(i \in J\), a sheaf of \(\mathcal{O}_{U_i}\)-modules \(\mathcal{M}_i\) on the chart \(U_i\);
212	    \item for each pair \((i,j)\), a \emph{transition isomorphism} of sheaves of
213	      modules on the overlap \(V_{ij}\),
214	      \[
215	        g_{ij} \;:\; f_{ij}^{*}\,\mathcal{M}_i
216	          \;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\;
217	        (t_{ij} \circ f_{ji})^{*}\,\mathcal{M}_j,
218	      \]
219	      comparing the two charts' sheaves after restriction (pullback) to the
220	      common overlap \(V_{ij}\);
221	  \end{itemize}
222	  \emph{subject to the following module cocycle conditions}, compatible with the
223	  scheme-level cocycle of \(D\) (its triple transition data \(t_{ij}\) and the
224	  overlap immersions \(f_{ij}\)), which are required as hypotheses on the family
225	  \((g_{ij})\):
226	  \begin{enumerate}
227	    \item[(C1)] \emph{self-identity:} the self-transition is the identity,
228	      \(g_{ii} = \mathrm{id}\) on \(f_{ii}^{*}\mathcal{M}_i\) (with
229	      \(V_{ii}\) and \(t_{ii}\) the diagonal data of \(D\));
230	    \item[(C2)] \emph{triple-overlap multiplicativity:} over each common triple
231	      overlap \(V_{ijk} = V_{ij} \times_{U_i} V_{ik}\) the base-change transports
232	      (\cref{lem:modules_pullback_basechange_transport}) of the three transition
233	      isomorphisms satisfy
234	      \[
235	        \widehat{g}_{jk}^{\,i} \circ \widehat{g}_{ij}^{\,k}
236	          \;=\; \widehat{g}_{ik}^{\,j},
237	      \]
238	      where \(\widehat{g}_{ij}^{\,k}\), \(\widehat{g}_{jk}^{\,i}\),
239	      \(\widehat{g}_{ik}^{\,j}\) are the transports of \(g_{ij}\), \(g_{jk}\),
240	      \(g_{ik}\) to \(V_{ijk}\) through the scheme-level triple transition
241	      \(t'_{ijk}\) and its compatibility \(t_{\mathrm{fac}}\), reassociated by the
242	      pullback comparison \cref{def:modules_pullbackComp} so that all three land on
243	      the same sheaf -- the multiplicative, \(\mathrm{GL}_d\)-valued cocycle
244	      condition.
245	  \end{enumerate}
246	  Conditions (C1) and (C2) are exactly the descent datum for the Zariski cover
247	  \(\{\iota_i\}\); it is subject to them that the gluing is well-defined. Out of this
248	  data the construction produces a glued sheaf of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules
249	  \(\mathcal{M}\) on the glued scheme \(X\).
250	
251	  \emph{Construction.} The category \(\ShMod(\mathcal{O}_X)\) of sheaves of
252	  \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules has all limits, and both pushforward along a morphism and
253	  these limits preserve the sheaf condition; the glued sheaf \(\mathcal{M}\) is therefore
254	  built directly as a limit -- an \emph{equalizer of pushforwards} -- rather than through a
255	  hand-built presheaf of compatible families. Concretely, form the two parallel maps of
256	  sheaves of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules
257	  \[
258	    \prod_{i} (\iota_i)_{*}\,\mathcal{M}_i
259	      \;\;\underset{b}{\overset{a}{\rightrightarrows}}\;\;
260	    \prod_{i,j} (\iota_i \circ f_{ij})_{*}\,\bigl(f_{ij}^{*}\mathcal{M}_i\bigr),
261	  \]
262	  where, on the \((i,j)\)-component, leg \(a\) pushes the \(i\)-th factor forward along the
263	  overlap immersion \(f_{ij}\) using the unit of the pullback--pushforward adjunction
264	  together with the pushforward-composition comparison
265	  \((\iota_i)_{*} \cong (\iota_i \circ f_{ij})_{*}\circ f_{ij}^{*}\) (the dual of
266	  \cref{def:modules_pullbackComp}), and leg \(b\) pushes the \(j\)-th factor forward and
267	  additionally transports it across the inverse transition isomorphism \((g_{ij})^{-1}\)
268	  and the glue datum's compatibility identity, so that both legs land on the common overlap
269	  sheaf \((\iota_i \circ f_{ij})_{*}(f_{ij}^{*}\mathcal{M}_i)\). The glued sheaf is the
270	  equalizer
271	  \[
272	    \mathcal{M} \;=\; \operatorname{eq}(a,b) \;\hookrightarrow\; \prod_{i} (\iota_i)_{*}\,\mathcal{M}_i,
273	  \]
274	  the sub-sheaf of tuples of pushed-forward chart sections whose two overlap restrictions
275	  agree -- precisely the compatible families, now realised as a categorical limit rather than
276	  a bespoke presheaf. The cocycle conditions (C1)/(C2) on the family \((g_{ij})\) are
277	  \emph{not} consumed in forming this equalizer object: the limit exists for any family of
278	  transition maps. They are instead what make the equalizer descend correctly, and are
279	  exactly the hypotheses pinned down downstream when the restriction isomorphisms
280	  \cref{def:gr_modules_glueRestrictionIso} are produced. This specialises effective descent
281	  of sheaves of modules to the concrete open cover \(\{\iota_i : U_i \hookrightarrow X\}\)
282	  supplied by a scheme glue datum, and is the form in which the Grassmannian's
283	  tautological bundle and quotient are built.
284	
285	  The glued sheaf comes with three further structures, each recorded as its own block
286	  downstream: the \emph{restriction isomorphisms} \(\rho_i : \iota_i^{*}\mathcal{M}
287	  \xrightarrow{\sim} \mathcal{M}_i\) compatible with the \(g_{ij}\) over the overlaps
288	  \cref{def:gr_modules_glueRestrictionIso}; the \emph{characterisation up to unique
289	  isomorphism} by the restriction property \cref{lem:gr_modules_glue_unique}; and the
290	  \emph{descent of morphisms} -- a family \(\phi_i : \mathcal{M}_i \to \mathcal{N}_i\)
291	  commuting with the transition isomorphisms glues to a unique \(\phi : \mathcal{M} \to
292	  \mathcal{N}\) with \(\rho_i^{\mathcal{N}} \circ \iota_i^{*}\phi = \phi_i \circ
293	  \rho_i^{\mathcal{M}}\) \cref{def:gr_modules_glueHom}.
294	\end{definition}
295	
296	\begin{definition}\leanok
297	[Restriction isomorphisms of the glued sheaf]
298	  \label{def:gr_modules_glueRestrictionIso}
299	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.glueRestrictionIso}
300	  % NOTE: forward declaration (planned work); the \lean{} target is not yet realised.
301	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_glue, lem:over_restrict_pullback_iso,
302	    lem:modules_pullback_basechange_transport, lem:gr_glueData_bridges}
303	  Let \(\mathcal{M}\) be the glued sheaf of \cref{def:scheme_modules_glue}. For each
304	  chart index \(i\) there is a \emph{restriction isomorphism} of sheaves of
305	  \(\mathcal{O}_{U_i}\)-modules
306	  \[
307	    \rho_i \;:\; \iota_i^{*}\,\mathcal{M} \;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\; \mathcal{M}_i,
308	  \]
309	  compatible with the transition isomorphisms: over each overlap \(V_{ij}\) the two
310	  restrictions \(\rho_i\) and \(\rho_j\), transported to the common overlap, differ
311	  exactly by \(g_{ij}\).
312	\end{definition}
313	\begin{proof}
314	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_glue, lem:over_restrict_pullback_iso,
315	    lem:modules_pullback_basechange_transport, lem:gr_glueData_bridges}
316	    \leanok
317	  By the equalizer-of-pushforwards description \cref{def:scheme_modules_glue}, a section of
318	  \(\mathcal{M}\) over an open of the form \(\iota_i^{-1}V\), restricted to the chart
319	  \(U_i\), is recovered from and recovers its \(i\)-th component
320	  \(s_i \in \Gamma(\mathcal{M}_i, \cdot)\) -- the \(i\)-th factor of the product
321	  \(\prod_i (\iota_i)_{*}\mathcal{M}_i\) into which the equalizer embeds; the
322	  chart-restriction bridge \cref{lem:over_restrict_pullback_iso} identifies the pullback
323	  \(\iota_i^{*}\mathcal{M}\) with this restriction, and the \(i\)-th projection
324	  \((s_\bullet) \mapsto s_i\) is the isomorphism \(\rho_i\) (with inverse extending a chart
325	  section by the overlap relation).
326	  The overlap compatibility is the defining relation of the compatible families: over
327	  \(V_{ij}\) the components \(s_i, s_j\) are related by \(g_{ij}\), which is precisely the
328	  statement that \(\rho_i\) and \(\rho_j\) differ by \(g_{ij}\) after transport to
329	  \(V_{ij}\); the triple-overlap coherence needed for this to be consistent is (C2),
330	  whose transports are aligned by \cref{lem:modules_pullback_basechange_transport} and
331	  \cref{lem:gr_glueData_bridges}.
332	\end{proof}
333	
334	\begin{lemma}[Uniqueness of the glued sheaf up to canonical isomorphism]
335	  \label{lem:gr_modules_glue_unique}
336	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.glue_unique}
337	  % NOTE: forward declaration (planned work); the \lean{} target is not yet realised.
338	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_glue, def:gr_modules_glueRestrictionIso,
339	    lem:sheaf_existsUnique_gluing_mathlib}
340	  Suppose \(\mathcal{M}'\) is another sheaf of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules equipped with
341	  isomorphisms \(\rho'_i : \iota_i^{*}\mathcal{M}' \xrightarrow{\sim} \mathcal{M}_i\)
342	  compatible with the \(g_{ij}\) over the overlaps. Then there is a unique isomorphism
343	  \(\mathcal{M} \xrightarrow{\sim} \mathcal{M}'\) intertwining the restriction
344	  isomorphisms, i.e.\ with \(\rho'_i \circ \iota_i^{*}(\cdot) = \rho_i\) for every \(i\).
345	\end{lemma}
346	\begin{proof}
347	  \uses{def:gr_modules_glueRestrictionIso, lem:sheaf_existsUnique_gluing_mathlib}
348	  A morphism of sheaves of modules on \(X\) is determined by, and may be assembled from,
349	  its restrictions to the members of the open cover \(\{\iota_i\}\), agreeing on the
350	  overlaps -- the locality and gluing of sections
351	  (\cref{lem:sheaf_existsUnique_gluing_mathlib}) applied to the internal hom. The
352	  intertwining condition forces the restriction over \(U_i\) to be
353	  \((\rho'_i)^{-1} \circ \rho_i : \iota_i^{*}\mathcal{M} \to \iota_i^{*}\mathcal{M}'\);
354	  these local isomorphisms agree on each overlap \(V_{ij}\) because both
355	  \((\rho_i)\) and \((\rho'_i)\) are compatible with the \emph{same} transition family
356	  \((g_{ij})\) (\cref{def:gr_modules_glueRestrictionIso}). By locality they glue to a
357	  unique global isomorphism \(\mathcal{M} \xrightarrow{\sim} \mathcal{M}'\) with the
358	  required property; uniqueness is the separation half of the same gluing.
359	\end{proof}
360	
361	\begin{definition}[Descent of morphisms along the cover]
362	  \label{def:gr_modules_glueHom}
363	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.glueHom}
364	  % NOTE: forward declaration (planned work); the \lean{} target is not yet realised.
365	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_glue, def:gr_modules_glueRestrictionIso,
366	    lem:sheaf_existsUnique_gluing_mathlib}
367	  Let \((\mathcal{M}_i, g_{ij})\) and \((\mathcal{N}_i, h_{ij})\) be two glued data
368	  over the same glue datum \(D\), with glued sheaves \(\mathcal{M}, \mathcal{N}\) and
369	  restriction isomorphisms \(\rho^{\mathcal{M}}_i, \rho^{\mathcal{N}}_i\). Given a family
370	  of \(\mathcal{O}_{U_i}\)-linear morphisms \(\phi_i : \mathcal{M}_i \to \mathcal{N}_i\)
371	  commuting with the transition isomorphisms on every overlap (i.e.\ \(h_{ij}\) intertwines
372	  the pullbacks of \(\phi_i\) and \(\phi_j\) over \(V_{ij}\)), there is a unique morphism
373	  of sheaves of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules \(\phi : \mathcal{M} \to \mathcal{N}\) with
374	  \[
375	    \rho^{\mathcal{N}}_i \circ \iota_i^{*}\phi \;=\; \phi_i \circ \rho^{\mathcal{M}}_i
376	    \qquad \text{for every } i.
377	  \]
378	\end{definition}
379	\begin{proof}
380	  \uses{def:gr_modules_glueRestrictionIso, lem:sheaf_existsUnique_gluing_mathlib}
381	  On each chart the prescribed identity defines a local morphism
382	  \((\rho^{\mathcal{N}}_i)^{-1} \circ \phi_i \circ \rho^{\mathcal{M}}_i :
383	  \iota_i^{*}\mathcal{M} \to \iota_i^{*}\mathcal{N}\). The commutation of the \(\phi_i\)
384	  with the transition isomorphisms \((g_{ij}, h_{ij})\), together with the overlap
385	  compatibility of the \(\rho\)-families (\cref{def:gr_modules_glueRestrictionIso}), makes
386	  these local morphisms agree on every overlap \(V_{ij}\). By the locality and gluing of
387	  sections (\cref{lem:sheaf_existsUnique_gluing_mathlib}) applied to the internal hom they
388	  promote to a unique global morphism \(\phi : \mathcal{M} \to \mathcal{N}\) restricting
389	  to each \(\phi_i\) through the \(\rho_i\) -- the descent of \(\mathrm{Hom}\) along the
390	  cover.
391	\end{proof}

393	\subsection{Pullback of free sheaves along an arbitrary morphism}
394	\label{sec:grquot_pullback_free}
395	
396	The functor of points (\cref{def:grassmannian_functor}) acts on morphisms by pullback, so
397	it needs the pullback of the trivial bundle to be again trivial --
398	\(\varphi^{*}\mathcal{O}_T^{\,\oplus I} \cong \mathcal{O}_{T'}^{\,\oplus I}\) -- for an
399	\emph{arbitrary} scheme morphism \(\varphi\). Mathlib's pullback-of-free comparison applies
400	only when the underlying site functor \(\mathrm{Opens.map}\,\varphi\) is \emph{final}; the
401	following three blocks remove that restriction for every morphism, and the resulting
402	pullback machinery preserves rank-\(d\) local freeness.
403	
404	\begin{lemma}\leanok
405	[The opens-pullback functor is final]
406	  \label{lem:gr_opensMap_final}
407	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.opensMap_final}
408	  For any morphism of schemes \(\varphi : T' \to T\), the inverse-image functor on opens
409	  \(\mathrm{Opens.map}\,\varphi : \mathrm{Opens}(T) \to \mathrm{Opens}(T')\),
410	  \(U \mapsto \varphi^{-1}U\), is \emph{final}.
411	\end{lemma}
412	
413	\begin{proof}
414	
415	\leanok
416	  Finality means that for every open \(V \subseteq T'\) the structured-arrow category of
417	  opens \(U \subseteq T\) with \(V \le \varphi^{-1}U\) is connected. That category is
418	  nonempty and has the terminal object \(U = \top\) -- one always has
419	  \(V \le \varphi^{-1}\top = T'\), and every object admits a unique arrow to \(\top\) --
420	  and a category with a terminal object is connected.
421	\end{proof}
422	
423	\begin{lemma}\leanok
424	[Pullback of a free sheaf is free]
425	  \label{lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso}
426	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso}
427	  \uses{lem:gr_opensMap_final}
428	  For any scheme morphism \(\varphi : T' \to T\) and index type \(I\), there is a canonical
429	  isomorphism of sheaves of \(\mathcal{O}_{T'}\)-modules
430	  \[
431	    \varphi^{*}\bigl(\mathcal{O}_T^{\,\oplus I}\bigr)
432	      \;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\;
433	    \mathcal{O}_{T'}^{\,\oplus I}.
434	  \]
435	\end{lemma}
436	
437	\begin{proof}
438	  \uses{lem:gr_opensMap_final}
439	  \leanok
440	  Mathlib's pullback-of-free comparison produces this isomorphism whenever the site functor
441	  \(\mathrm{Opens.map}\,\varphi\) is final; that finality is \cref{lem:gr_opensMap_final},
442	  which holds for every morphism, so the comparison applies unconditionally.
443	\end{proof}
444	
445	\begin{lemma}
446	
447	\leanok
448	[Free-pullback comparison is natural in the base morphism]
449	  \label{lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso_eqToHom}
450	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso_eqToHom}
451	  \uses{lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso}
452	  Let \(\varphi, \psi : T' \to T\) be morphisms with \(\varphi = \psi\) and \(I\) an index
453	  type. The two free-pullback comparisons \cref{lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso} along \(\varphi\) and
454	  \(\psi\) agree after transporting the differing pullback sources by the canonical
455	  \(\mathrm{eqToHom}\): the composite of that transport with
456	  \(\mathrm{pullbackFreeIso}(\psi)\) equals \(\mathrm{pullbackFreeIso}(\varphi)\).
457	\end{lemma}
458	\begin{proof}
459	  \uses{lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso}
460	  \leanok
461	  Substituting the equality \(\varphi = \psi\) collapses the \(\mathrm{eqToHom}\) to the
462	  identity and the two comparisons coincide.
463	\end{proof}
464	
465	\begin{lemma}
466	
467	\leanok
468	[Iso-level free-pullback cancellation]
469	  \label{lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso_trans_symm_eqToIso}
470	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso_trans_symm_eqToIso}
471	  \uses{lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso}
472	  For equal base morphisms \(\varphi = \psi : T' \to T\) and index type \(I\), the composite
473	  \(\mathrm{pullbackFreeIso}(\varphi) \circ \mathrm{pullbackFreeIso}(\psi)^{-1}\) is the
474	  \(\mathrm{eqToIso}\) transport between the (differing) pullback sources of the free sheaf
475	  \(\mathcal{O}_T^{\,\oplus I}\). Stated and proved generically in the base morphisms, so its
476	  use never forces evaluation of a concrete immersion; this is the load-bearing replacement
477	  for the cast chain in the self-identity \cref{lem:gr_bundleCocycle_id}.
478	\end{lemma}
479	\begin{proof}
480	  \uses{lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso}
481	  \leanok
482	  Substituting \(\varphi = \psi\) turns the composite into
483	  \(\mathrm{pullbackFreeIso}(\varphi) \circ \mathrm{pullbackFreeIso}(\varphi)^{-1} =
484	  \mathrm{id}\), which is the \(\mathrm{eqToIso}\) of the now-trivial source equality.
485	\end{proof}
486	
487	\begin{lemma}\leanok
488	[Pullback preserves rank-\(d\) local freeness]
489	  \label{lem:gr_pullback_isLocallyFreeOfRank}
490	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.pullback_isLocallyFreeOfRank}
491	  \uses{lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso, def:is_locally_free_of_rank, def:modules_pullbackComp}
492	  Let \(\varphi : T' \to T\) be a morphism of schemes and \(\mathcal{M}\) a sheaf of
493	  \(\mathcal{O}_T\)-modules locally free of rank \(d\)
494	  (\cref{def:is_locally_free_of_rank}). Then the pullback \(\varphi^{*}\mathcal{M}\) is
495	  locally free of rank \(d\) on \(T'\).
496	\end{lemma}
497	
498	\begin{proof}
499	  \uses{lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso, def:is_locally_free_of_rank, def:modules_pullbackComp}
500	  \leanok
501	  Choose an open cover \(\{U_i\}\) of \(T\) trivialising \(\mathcal{M}\),
502	  \(\mathcal{M}|_{U_i} \cong \mathcal{O}_{U_i}^{\,d}\). The preimages
503	  \(\{\varphi^{-1}U_i\}\) cover \(T'\). On each member the factorisation
504	  \(\varphi \circ \iota_{\varphi^{-1}U_i} = (\varphi|_{U_i}) \circ \iota_{U_i}\) of the
505	  restricted morphism, together with the pseudofunctor comparison of iterated pullbacks
506	  (\cref{def:modules_pullbackComp}), identifies
507	  \((\varphi^{*}\mathcal{M})|_{\varphi^{-1}U_i}\) with
508	  \((\varphi|_{U_i})^{*}\mathcal{O}_{U_i}^{\,d}\); the latter is
509	  \(\mathcal{O}_{\varphi^{-1}U_i}^{\,d}\) by \cref{lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso}. Hence
510	  \(\varphi^{*}\mathcal{M}\) is locally free of rank \(d\).
511	\end{proof}
512	
513	\section{The tautological quotient on the charts}
514	\label{sec:grquot_chart}
515	
516	\emph{Infrastructure: scalar endomorphisms.} Realising a matrix of regular
517	functions as a morphism of free sheaves of modules has no off-the-shelf primitive
518	in Mathlib; it is assembled from two small project-local helpers that turn a global
519	function into a scalar endomorphism of the structure sheaf, after which the matrix
520	is reconstituted entry-by-entry over a finite biproduct.
521	
522	\begin{definition}\leanok
523	[Global section of the unit module]
524	  \label{def:gr_globalUnitSection}
525	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.globalUnitSection}
526	  \uses{lem:moduleUnit_mathlib}
527	  Let \(X\) be a scheme and \(a \in \Gamma(X, \mathcal{O}_X)\) a global section of
528	  the structure sheaf. Viewing \(\mathcal{O}_X\) as the unit module
529	  \(\mathbf{1} \in \ShMod(\mathcal{O}_X)\) (\cref{lem:moduleUnit_mathlib}), the
530	  section \(a\) determines a global section of \(\mathbf{1}\): on each open
531	  \(V \subseteq X\) take the restriction \(a|_V \in \Gamma(V, \mathcal{O}_X)\), the
532	  family \((a|_V)_V\) being compatible with the restriction maps because
533	  restriction is functorial. This is the section of the unit sheaf attached to a
534	  global function.
535	\end{definition}
536	
537	\begin{definition}\leanok
538	[Scalar endomorphism of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)]
539	  \label{def:gr_scalarEnd}
540	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.scalarEnd}
541	  \uses{def:gr_globalUnitSection, lem:moduleUnit_mathlib}
542	  For \(a \in \Gamma(X, \mathcal{O}_X)\) the \emph{scalar endomorphism}
543	  \(\mathrm{scalarEnd}(a) : \mathcal{O}_X \to \mathcal{O}_X\) is the
544	  \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-linear endomorphism of the unit module given by multiplication
545	  by \(a\). It is obtained from the global section of \cref{def:gr_globalUnitSection}
546	  under the canonical identification of endomorphisms of the unit module
547	  \(\mathbf{1}\) with its global sections, \(\End(\mathbf{1}) \cong
548	  \Gamma(X, \mathbf{1})\); concretely, on each open \(V\) it acts as
549	  multiplication by \(a|_V\). These scalar endomorphisms are the matrix-entry
550	  building blocks of the chart quotient.
551	\end{definition}
552	
553	\begin{lemma}\leanok
554	[Scalar endomorphism of the unit is the identity]
555	  \label{lem:gr_scalarEnd_one}
556	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.scalarEnd_one}
557	  \uses{def:gr_scalarEnd}
558	  The scalar endomorphism attached to the unit function \(1 \in
559	  \Gamma(X, \mathcal{O}_X)\) is the identity of the unit module:
560	  \(\mathrm{scalarEnd}(1) = \mathrm{id}_{\mathbf{1}}\).
561	\end{lemma}
562	
563	\begin{proof}
564	  \uses{def:gr_scalarEnd}
565	  Multiplication by \(1\) is the identity on every section, so under the
566	  identification \(\End(\mathbf{1}) \cong \Gamma(X, \mathbf{1})\) the section \(1\)
567	  corresponds to \(\mathrm{id}_{\mathbf{1}}\).
568	\end{proof}
569	
570	\begin{lemma}\leanok
571	[Scalar endomorphism of the zero function is zero]
572	  \label{lem:gr_scalarEnd_zero}
573	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.scalarEnd_zero}
574	  \uses{def:gr_scalarEnd}
575	  The scalar endomorphism attached to the zero function \(0 \in
576	  \Gamma(X, \mathcal{O}_X)\) is the zero endomorphism:
577	  \(\mathrm{scalarEnd}(0) = 0\).
578	\end{lemma}
579	
580	\begin{proof}
581	  \uses{def:gr_scalarEnd}
582	  Multiplication by \(0\) sends every section to \(0\), so the corresponding
583	  endomorphism of the unit module is the zero map.
584	\end{proof}
585	
586	\begin{lemma}\leanok
587	[Composition of scalar endomorphisms is multiplication]
588	  \label{lem:gr_scalarEnd_comp}
589	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.scalarEnd_comp}
590	  \uses{def:gr_scalarEnd}
591	  For \(a, b \in \Gamma(X, \mathcal{O}_X)\) the scalar endomorphisms compose by
592	  multiplication: \(\mathrm{scalarEnd}(a) \circ \mathrm{scalarEnd}(b) =
593	  \mathrm{scalarEnd}(a\,b)\).
594	\end{lemma}
595	\begin{proof}
596	  \uses{def:gr_scalarEnd}
597	  Under the identification \(\End(\mathbf{1}) \cong \Gamma(X, \mathbf{1})\) the
598	  scalar endomorphisms act as multiplication by \(a\) and by \(b\); their composite
599	  is multiplication by \(a\,b\), because the section ring is commutative and
600	  restriction maps are ring homomorphisms.
601	\end{proof}
602	
603	\begin{lemma}\leanok
604	[Scalar endomorphism is additive]
605	  \label{lem:gr_scalarEnd_add}
606	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.scalarEnd_add}
607	  \uses{def:gr_scalarEnd}
608	  For \(a, b \in \Gamma(X, \mathcal{O}_X)\) one has \(\mathrm{scalarEnd}(a + b) =
609	  \mathrm{scalarEnd}(a) + \mathrm{scalarEnd}(b)\).
610	\end{lemma}
611	\begin{proof}
612	  \uses{def:gr_scalarEnd}
613	  Multiplication by \(a + b\) equals multiplication by \(a\) plus multiplication by
614	  \(b\) on every section, since the restriction maps of \(\mathcal{O}_X\) are additive;
615	  under \(\End(\mathbf{1}) \cong \Gamma(X, \mathbf{1})\) this is the stated additivity.
616	\end{proof}
617	
618	\begin{lemma}
619	
620	\leanok
621	[Scalar endomorphism of a finite sum]
622	  \label{lem:gr_scalarEnd_sum}
623	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.scalarEnd_sum}
624	  \uses{def:gr_scalarEnd, lem:gr_scalarEnd_zero, lem:gr_scalarEnd_add}
625	  For a finite index set \(s\) and a family \(f : \iota \to \Gamma(X, \mathcal{O}_X)\),
626	  \(\mathrm{scalarEnd}\bigl(\sum_{i \in s} f_i\bigr) = \sum_{i \in s} \mathrm{scalarEnd}(f_i)\).
627	\end{lemma}
628	\begin{proof}
629	  \uses{lem:gr_scalarEnd_zero, lem:gr_scalarEnd_add}
630	  Induction on the finite set \(s\): the empty case is \(\mathrm{scalarEnd}(0) = 0\)
631	  (\cref{lem:gr_scalarEnd_zero}) and the insertion step is additivity
632	  (\cref{lem:gr_scalarEnd_add}).
633	\end{proof}
634	
635	\emph{Infrastructure: matrix automorphisms of the free sheaf.} To realise the
636	\(\mathrm{GL}_d\) bundle transitions one must promote an invertible \(d \times d\)
637	matrix of global functions to an \emph{automorphism} of the free rank-\(d\) sheaf
638	\(\mathcal{O}_S^{\,d}\), exactly as the chart quotient \cref{def:gr_chart_quotient}
639	realises the universal matrix. The next blocks assemble this matrix-to-morphism
640	functor and record its two structural identities -- that it carries matrix
641	multiplication to composition and the identity matrix to the identity -- which are
642	the algebraic heart of the bundle cocycle.
643	
644	\begin{definition}\leanok
645	[Matrix endomorphism of the free sheaf]
646	  \label{def:gr_matrixEnd}
647	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.matrixEnd}
648	  \uses{def:gr_scalarEnd}
649	  Let \(S\) be a scheme, \(d \in \mathbb{N}\), and
650	  \(M \in \operatorname{Mat}_{d \times d}(\Gamma(S, \mathcal{O}_S))\) a matrix of
651	  global functions. The \emph{matrix endomorphism} \(\mathrm{matrixEnd}(M)\) is the
652	  endomorphism of the free rank-\(d\) sheaf \(\mathcal{O}_S^{\,d}\) whose
653	  \((p,q)\)-component, under the presentation of \(\mathcal{O}_S^{\,d}\) as the
654	  finite biproduct of \(d\) copies of the unit module \(\mathbf{1}\), is the scalar
655	  endomorphism \(\mathrm{scalarEnd}(M_{p,q})\) (\cref{def:gr_scalarEnd}). It is
656	  assembled from these components by the universal property of the rank-\(d\)
657	  biproduct, exactly as the chart quotient \(u^I\) is assembled
658	  (\cref{def:gr_chart_quotient}). This is the substitute for the (non-existent)
659	  Mathlib ``matrix \(\mapsto\) endomorphism of a free sheaf'' primitive.
660	\end{definition}
661	
662	\begin{lemma}\leanok
663	[Matrix endomorphism carries multiplication to composition]
664	  \label{lem:gr_matrixEnd_comp}
665	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.matrixEnd_comp}
666	  \uses{def:gr_matrixEnd, lem:gr_scalarEnd_comp, lem:gr_scalarEnd_add}
667	  For \(M, N \in \operatorname{Mat}_{d \times d}(\Gamma(S, \mathcal{O}_S))\) the matrix
668	  endomorphisms compose according to the matrix product, with the order reversed by
669	  the contravariance of the component indexing:
670	  \[
671	    \mathrm{matrixEnd}(M) \,\circ\, \mathrm{matrixEnd}(N)
672	      \;=\; \mathrm{matrixEnd}(N \cdot M).
673	  \]
674	\end{lemma}
675	\begin{proof}
676	  \uses{def:gr_matrixEnd, lem:gr_scalarEnd_comp, lem:gr_scalarEnd_add}
677	  The composition of two biproduct-assembled morphisms is again biproduct-assembled,
678	  with \((i,q)\)-component the sum over the middle index \(p\) of the composites of
679	  components, \(\sum_p \mathrm{component}_{i,p} \circ \mathrm{component}_{p,q}\) (the
680	  categorical matrix product over the biproduct). Each composite of scalar
681	  endomorphisms is the scalar endomorphism of the product,
682	  \(\mathrm{scalarEnd}(a)\circ\mathrm{scalarEnd}(b) = \mathrm{scalarEnd}(ab)\)
683	  (\cref{lem:gr_scalarEnd_comp}), and a finite sum of scalar endomorphisms is the
684	  scalar endomorphism of the sum (\cref{lem:gr_scalarEnd_add}); hence the \((i,q)\)-component
685	  of the composite is \(\mathrm{scalarEnd}\bigl(\sum_p M_{q,p} N_{p,i}\bigr) =
686	  \mathrm{scalarEnd}\bigl((N\cdot M)_{q,i}\bigr)\), which is precisely the
687	  \((i,q)\)-component of \(\mathrm{matrixEnd}(N\cdot M)\). The reversal of the factor
688	  order is the contravariance of the column/component indexing.
689	\end{proof}
690	
691	\begin{lemma}\leanok
692	[Matrix endomorphism of the identity is the identity]
693	  \label{lem:gr_matrixEnd_one}
694	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.matrixEnd_one}
695	  \uses{def:gr_matrixEnd, lem:gr_scalarEnd_one, lem:gr_scalarEnd_zero}
696	  The matrix endomorphism of the identity matrix is the identity morphism of
697	  \(\mathcal{O}_S^{\,d}\): \(\mathrm{matrixEnd}(1_{d\times d}) =
698	  \mathrm{id}_{\mathcal{O}_S^{\,d}}\).
699	\end{lemma}
700	\begin{proof}
701	  \uses{def:gr_matrixEnd, lem:gr_scalarEnd_one, lem:gr_scalarEnd_zero}
702	  The diagonal components are \(\mathrm{scalarEnd}(1) = \mathrm{id}\)
703	  (\cref{lem:gr_scalarEnd_one}) and the off-diagonal components are
704	  \(\mathrm{scalarEnd}(0) = 0\) (\cref{lem:gr_scalarEnd_zero}); the morphism with this
705	  component matrix is the identity of the biproduct.
706	\end{proof}
707	
708	\begin{definition}\leanok
709	[Free-sheaf automorphism of an invertible matrix]
710	  \label{def:gr_matrixToFreeIso}
711	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.matrixToFreeIso}
712	  \uses{def:gr_matrixEnd, lem:gr_matrixEnd_comp, lem:gr_matrixEnd_one}
713	  Let \(M, N \in \operatorname{Mat}_{d \times d}(\Gamma(S, \mathcal{O}_S))\) be mutually
714	  inverse, \(M N = 1\) and \(N M = 1\). The \emph{matrix automorphism}
715	  \(\mathrm{matrixToFreeIso}(M, N)\) is the isomorphism of free sheaves
716	  \(\mathcal{O}_S^{\,d} \xrightarrow{\sim} \mathcal{O}_S^{\,d}\) with forward map
717	  \(\mathrm{matrixEnd}(M)\) and inverse map \(\mathrm{matrixEnd}(N)\); the two
718	  composites are identities by \cref{lem:gr_matrixEnd_comp} (turning the products
719	  \(NM\) and \(MN\) into the relevant compositions) and \cref{lem:gr_matrixEnd_one}.
720	  Its forward map is \(\mathrm{matrixEnd}(M)\) by construction
721	  (\cref{lem:gr_matrixToFreeIso_hom}).
722	\end{definition}
723	
724	\begin{lemma}\leanok
725	[Forward map of the matrix automorphism]
726	  \label{lem:gr_matrixToFreeIso_hom}
727	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.matrixToFreeIso_hom}
728	  \uses{def:gr_matrixToFreeIso, def:gr_matrixEnd}
729	  The forward map of \(\mathrm{matrixToFreeIso}(M, N)\) is \(\mathrm{matrixEnd}(M)\).
730	\end{lemma}
731	\begin{proof}
732	  \uses{def:gr_matrixToFreeIso, def:gr_matrixEnd}
733	  Immediate from the definition \cref{def:gr_matrixToFreeIso}, whose forward field is
734	  literally \(\mathrm{matrixEnd}(M)\).
735	\end{proof}
736	
737	\begin{lemma}\leanok
738	[Matrix automorphisms compose multiplicatively]
739	  \label{lem:gr_matrixToFreeIso_mul}
740	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.matrixToFreeIso_mul}
741	  \uses{def:gr_matrixToFreeIso, lem:gr_matrixToFreeIso_hom, lem:gr_matrixEnd_comp}
742	  Let \(A, A', B, B'\) be \(d \times d\) matrices of global functions on \(S\) with
743	  \(A A' = A' A = 1\), \(B B' = B' B = 1\), so that \(\mathrm{matrixToFreeIso}(A, A')\)
744	  and \(\mathrm{matrixToFreeIso}(B, B')\) are automorphisms of \(\mathcal{O}_S^{\,d}\).
745	  Their forward maps compose to the forward map of the product automorphism, with the
746	  order reversed by the component contravariance:
747	  \[
748	    \mathrm{matrixToFreeIso}(A, A')_{\mathrm{hom}}
749	      \,\circ\, \mathrm{matrixToFreeIso}(B, B')_{\mathrm{hom}}
750	      \;=\; \mathrm{matrixEnd}(B \cdot A).
751	  \]
752	  Equivalently, composition of matrix automorphisms realises matrix multiplication of
753	  the underlying matrices. This is the linkage that turns the matrix-level cocycle
754	  identity into a composition identity of sheaf-of-module isomorphisms.
755	\end{lemma}
756	\begin{proof}
757	  \uses{def:gr_matrixToFreeIso, lem:gr_matrixToFreeIso_hom, lem:gr_matrixEnd_comp}
758	  By \cref{lem:gr_matrixToFreeIso_hom} the two forward maps are
759	  \(\mathrm{matrixEnd}(A)\) and \(\mathrm{matrixEnd}(B)\); their composite is
760	  \(\mathrm{matrixEnd}(A)\circ\mathrm{matrixEnd}(B) = \mathrm{matrixEnd}(B\cdot A)\) by
761	  \cref{lem:gr_matrixEnd_comp}.
762	\end{proof}
763	
764	\begin{definition}
765	
766	\leanok
767	[Chart quotient \(u^I\)]
768	  \label{def:gr_chart_quotient}
769	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.chartQuotientMap}
770	  \uses{def:gr_affine_chart, def:gr_universal_matrix, def:gr_scalarEnd}
771	  % SOURCE: [Nitsure], "Construction of Hilbert and Quot Schemes", \S 1,
772	  % sub-section "Universal quotient" (read from
773	  % references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L898-L910).
774	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
775	  % "We next define a rank $d$ locally free sheaf $\U$ on $\grass(r,d)$
776	  %  together with a surjective homomorphism $\oplus^r\,{\mathcal O}_{\grass(r,d)} \to \U$.
777	  %  On each $U^I$ we define a surjective homomorphism
778	  %  $u^I :\oplus^r\,{\mathcal O}_{U^I} \to  \oplus^d\,{\mathcal O}_{U^I}$
779	  %  by the matrix $X^I$."
780	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S 1.}
781	  Let \(I \subseteq \{1,\dots,r\}\) with \(\#(I) = d\). On the affine chart
782	  \(U^I = \Spec R^I\) (\cref{def:gr_affine_chart}) define the
783	  \(\mathcal{O}_{U^I}\)-linear homomorphism of trivial bundles
784	  \[
785	    u^I \;:\; \mathcal{O}_{U^I}^{\,r}
786	      \;\longrightarrow\; \mathcal{O}_{U^I}^{\,d}
787	  \]
788	  given by left multiplication by the universal \(d \times r\) matrix \(X^I\)
789	  (\cref{def:gr_universal_matrix}): on global sections it is the \(R^I\)-linear
790	  map \((R^I)^{r} \to (R^I)^{d}\), \(v \mapsto X^I v\). Since the \(I\)-th minor
791	  \(X^I_I\) of \(X^I\) is the identity matrix \(1_{d \times d}\), the composite of
792	  \(u^I\) with the inclusion of the \(I\)-indexed coordinates
793	  \(\mathcal{O}_{U^I}^{\,d} \hookrightarrow \mathcal{O}_{U^I}^{\,r}\) is the
794	  identity; hence \(u^I\) is a split surjection of locally free sheaves, with
795	  target the free rank-\(d\) sheaf \(\mathcal{O}_{U^I}^{\,d}\).
796	
797	  \emph{Realisation.} The free sheaves \(\mathcal{O}_{U^I}^{\,r}\) and
798	  \(\mathcal{O}_{U^I}^{\,d}\) are the finite biproducts (coproducts) of copies of
799	  the unit module \(\mathbf{1} = \mathcal{O}_{U^I}\), indexed by \(\{1,\dots,r\}\)
800	  and \(\{1,\dots,d\}\). Each entry \(X^I_{p,q} \in R^I\) of the universal matrix
801	  (\cref{def:gr_universal_matrix}) is a global function on \(U^I = \Spec R^I\),
802	  obtained from \(R^I\) through the canonical identification
803	  \(\Gamma(\Spec R^I, \mathcal{O}) \cong R^I\); the corresponding scalar
804	  endomorphism \(\mathrm{scalarEnd}(X^I_{p,q}) : \mathbf{1} \to \mathbf{1}\)
805	  (\cref{def:gr_scalarEnd}) is the \((p,q)\)-component of \(u^I\). Assembling these
806	  components over the index biproducts -- the universal property of the biproduct
807	  packaging a matrix of component morphisms into a single morphism of the free
808	  sheaves -- yields \(u^I\). This entry-by-entry assembly via the finite biproduct
809	  is the substitute for a (non-existent) Mathlib ``matrix \(\mapsto\) morphism of
810	  free sheaves'' primitive.
811	\end{definition}
812	
813	\begin{lemma}\leanok
814	[The $I$-columns of the chart quotient are the identity]
815	  \label{lem:gr_chartQuotientMap_iFree}
816	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.chartQuotientMap_ιFree}
817	  \uses{def:gr_chart_quotient, def:gr_universal_matrix, def:gr_scalarEnd,
818	    lem:gr_scalarEnd_one, lem:gr_scalarEnd_zero,
819	    lem:gr_universalMatrix_submatrix_self}
820	  For each \(k \in \{1,\dots,d\}\), the chart quotient \(u^I\)
821	  (\cref{def:gr_chart_quotient}) carries the \(\iota_I(k)\)-th basis section of the
822	  free sheaf \(\mathcal{O}_{U^I}^{\,r}\) -- where \(\iota_I(k)\) is the \(k\)-th
823	  element of \(I\) in increasing order -- to the \(k\)-th basis section of
824	  \(\mathcal{O}_{U^I}^{\,d}\); that is, the \(I\)-indexed columns of \(u^I\) form the
825	  identity:
826	  \[
827	    e_{\iota_I(k)} \,\circ\, u^I \;=\; e_k.
828	  \]
829	\end{lemma}
830	
831	\begin{proof}
832	  \uses{def:gr_chart_quotient, def:gr_universal_matrix, def:gr_scalarEnd,
833	    lem:gr_scalarEnd_one, lem:gr_scalarEnd_zero,
834	    lem:gr_universalMatrix_submatrix_self}
835	  By the biproduct presentation of \(u^I\) (\cref{def:gr_chart_quotient}), the
836	  composite \(e_{\iota_I(k)} \circ u^I\) reads off the \(\iota_I(k)\)-th column of the
837	  component matrix, whose \(p\)-th entry is the scalar endomorphism
838	  \(\mathrm{scalarEnd}(X^I_{p,\iota_I(k)})\). The \(I\)-indexed columns of the
839	  universal matrix \(X^I\), read through the order isomorphism \(\{1,\dots,d\} \cong
840	  I\), form the \(d \times d\) identity (\cref{lem:gr_universalMatrix_submatrix_self}),
841	  so \(X^I_{p,\iota_I(k)} = \delta_{p,k}\). Hence the diagonal entry is
842	  \(\mathrm{scalarEnd}(1) = \mathrm{id}\) (\cref{lem:gr_scalarEnd_one}) and the
843	  off-diagonal entries are \(\mathrm{scalarEnd}(0) = 0\)
844	  (\cref{lem:gr_scalarEnd_zero}); the resulting column is the \(k\)-th coordinate
845	  inclusion, giving \(e_{\iota_I(k)} \circ u^I = e_k\).
846	\end{proof}
847	
848	\begin{lemma}\leanok
849	[The chart quotient is an epimorphism]
850	  \label{lem:gr_chartQuotientMap_epi}
851	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.chartQuotientMap_epi}
852	  \uses{def:gr_chart_quotient}
853	  The chart quotient \(u^I : \mathcal{O}_{U^I}^{\,r} \to \mathcal{O}_{U^I}^{\,d}\)
854	  (\cref{def:gr_chart_quotient}) is an epimorphism of sheaves of modules.
855	\end{lemma}
856	
857	\begin{proof}
858	  \uses{def:gr_chart_quotient, lem:gr_chartQuotientMap_iFree}
859	  We exhibit a section, so that \(u^I\) is a \emph{split} epimorphism and hence an
860	  epimorphism. Let \(\iota_I : \{1,\dots,d\} \hookrightarrow \{1,\dots,r\}\) be the
861	  inclusion of the \(I\)-indexed coordinates, \(k \mapsto \iota_I(k)\), the
862	  \(k\)-th element of \(I\) in increasing order. It induces a morphism of free
863	  sheaves \(s_I : \mathcal{O}_{U^I}^{\,d} \to \mathcal{O}_{U^I}^{\,r}\), the
864	  coordinate inclusion sending the \(k\)-th basis section to the
865	  \(\iota_I(k)\)-th. We claim
866	  \[
867	    u^I \circ s_I \;=\; \mathrm{id}_{\mathcal{O}_{U^I}^{\,d}}.
868	  \]
869	  Both sides are morphisms out of the free rank-\(d\) sheaf, which is the coproduct
870	  of \(d\) copies of the unit module; by the universal property of that coproduct
871	  it suffices to check the equality on each coordinate, i.e.\ to compare component
872	  matrices. The \((p,k)\)-component of \(u^I \circ s_I\) is the entry
873	  \(X^I_{p,\iota_I(k)}\) of the universal matrix at the \(I\)-indexed column, which
874	  is precisely the \(I\)-th minor \(X^I_I\) of \(X^I\). Since that minor is the
875	  \(d \times d\) identity (the columns of \(X^I\) indexed by \(I\), read through the
876	  order isomorphism \(\{1,\dots,d\} \cong I\), form the identity block --
877	  \cref{def:gr_universal_matrix}), one has \(X^I_{p,\iota_I(k)} = \delta_{p,k}\),
878	  so \(u^I \circ s_I\) has the identity component matrix and equals
879	  \(\mathrm{id}\). Hence \(s_I\) splits \(u^I\) and \(u^I\) is a split
880	  epimorphism, in particular an epimorphism.
881	\end{proof}

883	\section{The \(\mathrm{GL}_d\) bundle transition cocycle}
884	\label{sec:grquot_bundle_cocycle}
885	
886	The universal quotient sheaf \(\mathcal{U}\) (\cref{def:gr_universal_quotient_sheaf}) is
887	obtained by feeding the gluing primitive \cref{def:scheme_modules_glue} the per-chart free
888	rank-\(d\) sheaves \(\mathcal{O}_{U^I}^{\,d}\), the bundle transitions
889	\(g_{I,J} = (X^I_J)^{-1}\), and the two module cocycle hypotheses (C1)/(C2) that primitive
890	requires. The matrix cocycle for the Cramer inverses \((X^I_J)^{-1}\) is already in hand --
891	it is the content of the scheme-level cocycle \cref{lem:gr_cocycle} and the minor identities
892	\cref{lem:gr_universalMinorInv_identities} -- but the gluing primitive consumes its
893	\emph{module-level} incarnation: an honest isomorphism of sheaves of modules together with
894	its self-identity and triple-overlap multiplicativity. The three blocks of this section
895	package exactly that, decoupling the construction of \(\mathcal{U}\) from the remaining
896	matrix-to-module transport bookkeeping. This is the only net-new infrastructure the
897	universal quotient requires beyond the gluing primitive itself.

988	\begin{lemma}\leanok
989	[Cramer-inverse cocycle on the triple overlap]
990	  \label{lem:gr_bundleCocycle_matrix}
991	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.bundleTransition_cocycle_matrix}
992	  \uses{def:gr_universalMinorInv, lem:gr_cocycle_imageMatrix_eq,
993	    lem:gr_universalMinorInv_identities, lem:gr_mul_submatrix_col,
994	    lem:gr_inv_mul_inv_mul_cancel, lem:gr_cocycle}
995	  Over the common triple-overlap ring (the localisation carrying the three charts'
996	  data to \(V_{IJK}\)) the Cramer inverses of the localised minors satisfy the
997	  multiplicative cocycle identity
998	  \[
999	    (X^J_K)^{-1}\,(X^I_J)^{-1} \;=\; (X^I_K)^{-1}.
1000	  \]
1001	  This is the pure matrix-algebra core of (C2), independent of any sheaf data.
1002	\end{lemma}
1003	\begin{proof}
1004	  \uses{def:gr_universalMinorInv, lem:gr_cocycle_imageMatrix_eq,
1005	    lem:gr_universalMinorInv_identities, lem:gr_mul_submatrix_col,
1006	    lem:gr_inv_mul_inv_mul_cancel, lem:gr_cocycle}
1007	  Write all matrices over the common triple-overlap ring. The image matrix of the
1008	  transition \(\theta_{I,J}\) is \(X^J = (X^I_J)^{-1} X^I\), and its \(K\)-minor is
1009	  computed by restricting to the \(K\)-indexed columns; since taking a column
1010	  submatrix commutes with left multiplication (\cref{lem:gr_mul_submatrix_col}),
1011	  \[
1012	    X^J_K \;=\; \bigl((X^I_J)^{-1} X^I\bigr)_K \;=\; (X^I_J)^{-1}\, X^I_K .
1013	  \]
1014	  Both \(X^I_J\) and \(X^I_K\) have unit determinant
1015	  (\cref{lem:gr_universalMinorInv_identities}), so this product of invertibles inverts
1016	  by \((AB)^{-1} = B^{-1} A^{-1}\):
1017	  \[
1018	    (X^J_K)^{-1} \;=\; (X^I_K)^{-1}\,(X^I_J).
1019	  \]
1020	  Multiplying on the right by \((X^I_J)^{-1}\) and cancelling \(X^I_J (X^I_J)^{-1} = 1\)
1021	  via the two-sided Cramer identity (\cref{lem:gr_universalMinorInv_identities}, the
1022	  cancellation packaged in \cref{lem:gr_inv_mul_inv_mul_cancel}) gives
1023	  \((X^J_K)^{-1} (X^I_J)^{-1} = (X^I_K)^{-1}\). The agreement of the three image
1024	  matrices over the triple overlap that licenses these manipulations is the content of
1025	  \cref{lem:gr_cocycle_imageMatrix_eq}, the matrix form of the scheme-level cocycle
1026	  \cref{lem:gr_cocycle}.
1027	\end{proof}

1031	  \label{lem:gr_scalarEnd_pullback}

1033	  \uses{def:gr_scalarEnd, lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso, def:modules_pullbackComp}
1034	  Let \(p : T \to S\) be a morphism of schemes and \(a \in \Gamma(S, \mathcal{O}_S)\) a
1035	  global function, with comorphism on global sections
1036	  \(p^{\sharp} : \Gamma(S, \mathcal{O}_S) \to \Gamma(T, \mathcal{O}_T)\).
1037	  Write \(q : p^{*}\mathbf{1} \xrightarrow{\sim} \mathbf{1}\) for the unit-pullback comparison
1038	  (\cref{lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso} at a one-element index). Then the pullback of the scalar endomorphism
1039	  \(\mathrm{scalarEnd}(a)\) is conjugate, through \(q\), to the scalar endomorphism of the
1040	  base-changed function:
1041	  \[
1042	    p^{*}\bigl(\mathrm{scalarEnd}(a)\bigr)
1043	      \;=\; q^{-1} \,\circ\, \mathrm{scalarEnd}\bigl(p^{\sharp} a\bigr) \,\circ\, q
1044	  \]
1045	  as endomorphisms of \(p^{*}\mathbf{1}\); equivalently
1046	  \(q \circ p^{*}(\mathrm{scalarEnd}(a)) = \mathrm{scalarEnd}(p^{\sharp} a) \circ q\). This is the
1047	  single-entry naturality atom underlying the matrix-level statement
1048	  \cref{lem:gr_matrixEnd_pullback}.
1049	\end{lemma}
1050	\begin{proof}
1051	  \uses{def:gr_scalarEnd, lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso}
1052	  The scalar endomorphism \(\mathrm{scalarEnd}(a)\) (\cref{def:gr_scalarEnd}) is multiplication
1053	  by the global section \(a\) on the unit module \(\mathbf 1 = \mathcal{O}_S\). Pulling back
1054	  along \(p\) sends multiplication by \(a\) to multiplication by its image, and the comorphism
1055	  \(p^{\sharp}\) is precisely the action of pullback on global functions, so
1056	  \(p^{*}(\mathrm{scalarEnd}(a))\) is multiplication by \(p^{\sharp}a\) on \(p^{*}\mathbf 1\). The
1057	  comparison \(q\) is an isomorphism of unit modules intertwining this multiplication on
1058	  \(p^{*}\mathbf 1\) with multiplication by \(p^{\sharp}a\) on \(\mathbf 1\); conjugating back
1059	  along \(q\) therefore yields the claimed identity. The verification is a single computation on
1060	  the unit module, where multiplication by a function commutes with the natural comparison
1061	  \(q\).
1062	\end{proof}

1193	  \uses{def:gr_the_glue_data, def:gr_chart_transition', def:gr_cocycle_theta_ij,
1194	    def:gr_away_pullback_iso, def:gr_away_mul_comm_equiv,
1195	    lem:gr_baseChange_bridge_gammaSpec}
1196	  With the notation of \cref{lem:gr_baseChange_bridge_left}, let
1197	  \(t'_{IJK} : V_{IJK} \to U^J_K \times_{U^J} U^J_I\) be the triple transition of the glue datum
1198	  (\cref{def:gr_chart_transition'}). Through the source and target away-pullback identifications
1199	  (\cref{def:gr_away_pullback_iso}) and the order-swap isomorphism
1200	  (\cref{def:gr_away_mul_comm_equiv}), \(t'_{IJK}\) is the \(\Spec\) morphism of the localised
1201	  triple-overlap transition \(\Theta_{IJ} : S_J \to S_I\) (\cref{def:gr_cocycle_theta_ij},
1202	  composed with the order-swap ring isomorphism). Consequently the global-sections base-change
1203	  map of \(t'_{IJK}\), transported through the affine global-sections identification, equals the
1204	  ring homomorphism \(\Theta_{IJ}\) (up to the order-swap); in particular the entrywise image of
1205	  the Cramer inverse \((X^J_K)^{-1}\) under the geometric base-change agrees with its image under
1206	  \(\Theta_{IJ}\).
1207	\end{lemma}
1208	\begin{proof}
1209	  \uses{def:gr_chart_transition', def:gr_cocycle_theta_ij, def:gr_away_mul_comm_equiv,
1210	    lem:gr_baseChange_bridge_gammaSpec}
1211	  By \cref{def:gr_chart_transition'} the triple transition \(t'_{IJK}\) is the composite of the
1212	  source away-pullback identification, \(\Spec\Theta_{IJ}\) (\cref{def:gr_cocycle_theta_ij}), the
1213	  \(\Spec\) of the order-swap isomorphism (\cref{def:gr_away_mul_comm_equiv}), and the inverse
1214	  target away-pullback identification. The charts and overlaps being affine, applying
1215	  \cref{lem:gr_baseChange_bridge_gammaSpec} to each \(\Spec\) factor identifies the global-sections
1216	  comorphism of \(t'_{IJK}\), through the affine global-sections identifications and the order-swap,
1217	  with the ring homomorphism \(\Theta_{IJ}\). The entrywise image of \((X^J_K)^{-1}\) under this
1218	  comorphism therefore agrees with its image under \(\Theta_{IJ}\).
1219	\end{proof}

1221	\begin{lemma}\leanok
1222	[Base-change bridge to the localised cocycle ring homomorphisms]
1223	  \label{lem:gr_baseChange_bridge}

1229	  Fix indices \(I, J, K\) and the triple overlap \(V_{IJK}\) of the chart cover
1230	  \(\{U^I\}\) of \(\mathrm{Gr}(r,d)\) (\cref{def:gr_glued_scheme}, \cref{def:gr_the_glue_data}).
1231	  The scheme-pullback base-change map on global sections
1232	  \(\Gamma(U^I_J, \mathcal{O}) \to \Gamma(V_{IJK}, \mathcal{O})\) induced by the first projection
1233	  \(p^{IJ}_{IJK}\), and likewise the maps induced by the second projection and by the triple
1234	  transition \(t'_{IJK}\) (\cref{def:gr_transition}), are identified -- through the natural identification of global sections of an affine scheme
1235	  with its coordinate ring and the fact that the projections and triple transition are the
1236	  \(\Spec\) morphisms of the coordinate-ring homomorphisms by which the Grassmannian glue datum
1237	  (\cref{def:gr_the_glue_data}) is assembled -- with the ring homomorphisms
1238	  \(\mathrm{awayInclLeft}\) (\cref{def:gr_away_incl_left}),
1239	  \(\mathrm{awayInclRight}\) (\cref{def:gr_away_incl_right}) and \(\Theta_{IJ}\)
1240	  (\cref{def:gr_cocycle_theta_ij}) over which the matrix cocycle
1241	  \cref{lem:gr_bundleCocycle_matrix} is stated. Consequently the entrywise image of a Cramer
1242	  inverse \((X^I_J)^{-1}\) under the geometric base-change map agrees with its image under the
1243	  corresponding coordinate-ring homomorphism, so \cref{lem:gr_bundleCocycle_matrix} applies
1244	  to the base-changed matrices.
1245	\end{lemma}
1246	\begin{proof}
1247	  \uses{lem:gr_baseChange_bridge_left, lem:gr_baseChange_bridge_right,
1248	    lem:gr_baseChange_bridge_transition}
1249	  The three identifications are exactly the three projection bridges. The first projection is
1250	  handled by \cref{lem:gr_baseChange_bridge_left}, identifying its global-sections base-change
1251	  map with \(\mathrm{awayInclLeft}\); the second projection by
1252	  \cref{lem:gr_baseChange_bridge_right}, identifying it with \(\mathrm{awayInclRight}\); and the
1253	  triple transition \(t'_{IJK}\) by \cref{lem:gr_baseChange_bridge_transition}, identifying it
1254	  with \(\Theta_{IJ}\). Each bridge already records the agreement of the entrywise image of the
1255	  relevant Cramer inverse under the geometric base-change with its image under the named ring
1256	  homomorphism over which \cref{lem:gr_bundleCocycle_matrix} is stated, so assembling the three
1257	  gives the claim.
1258	\end{proof}

1260	\begin{lemma}\leanok
1261	[Transport and endpoint alignment of the bundle transitions]
1262	  \label{lem:gr_bundleCocycle_transport}
1263	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.bundleTransition_cocycle_transport}
1264	  \uses{def:gr_bundleTransition, lem:gr_bundleCocycle_matrix, lem:gr_matrixToFreeIso_mul,
1265	    lem:gr_matrixEnd_pullback, lem:gr_baseChange_bridge,
1266	    lem:modules_pullback_basechange_transport, lem:gr_glueData_bridges,
1267	    def:modules_pullbackComp, lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso}
1268	  Fix indices \(I, J, K\) with triple overlap \(V_{IJK} = U^I_J \times_{U^I} U^I_K\),
1269	  and let \(\widehat{g}_{IJ}^{\,K}, \widehat{g}_{JK}^{\,I}, \widehat{g}_{IK}^{\,J}\) be
1270	  the base-change transports (\cref{lem:modules_pullback_basechange_transport}) of the
1271	  three bundle transitions to \(V_{IJK}\). Under the free-pullback comparisons
1272	  (\cref{lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso}) and the endpoint bridges
1273	  (\cref{lem:gr_glueData_bridges}), each transported transition is identified with the
1274	  matrix automorphism of the corresponding base-changed Cramer inverse on the common
1275	  free sheaf \(\mathcal{O}_{V_{IJK}}^{\,d}\); granting the matrix identity
1276	  \cref{lem:gr_bundleCocycle_matrix}, the composite satisfies
1277	  \[
1278	    \widehat{g}_{JK}^{\,I} \circ \widehat{g}_{IJ}^{\,K}
1279	      \;=\; \widehat{g}_{IK}^{\,J}
1280	  \]
1281	  as isomorphisms of sheaves of modules on \(V_{IJK}\). This is the substantive
1282	  transport step: the alignment of the three transports' domains and codomains.

1284	\begin{proof}
1285	  \uses{def:gr_bundleTransition, lem:gr_bundleCocycle_matrix, lem:gr_matrixToFreeIso_mul,
1286	    lem:gr_matrixEnd_pullback, lem:gr_baseChange_bridge,
1287	    lem:modules_pullback_basechange_transport, lem:gr_glueData_bridges,
1288	    def:modules_pullbackComp, lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso}

1290	  bundle transition is, by \cref{def:gr_bundleTransition}, \(\mathrm{matrixToFreeIso}\) of a
1291	  localised Cramer inverse conjugated by the free-pullback comparisons
1292	  \cref{lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso}; its base-change transport
1293	  (\cref{lem:modules_pullback_basechange_transport}) reassociates the iterated pullbacks via
1294	  \cref{def:modules_pullbackComp}, and the three endpoint bridges \cref{lem:gr_glueData_bridges}
1295	  pin the source, middle and target sheaves to a single \(\mathcal{O}_{V_{IJK}}^{\,d}\). By
1296	  \cref{lem:gr_matrixEnd_pullback} each transport then equals \(\mathrm{matrixEnd}\) of the
1297	  base-changed Cramer inverse, and by \cref{lem:gr_baseChange_bridge} those base-changed matrices
1298	  are exactly \((X^I_J)^{-1}, (X^J_K)^{-1}, (X^I_K)^{-1}\) over the common triple-overlap ring of
1299	  \cref{lem:gr_bundleCocycle_matrix}. The linkage \cref{lem:gr_matrixToFreeIso_mul} composes the
1300	  first two to \(\mathrm{matrixEnd}\bigl((X^J_K)^{-1}(X^I_J)^{-1}\bigr)\), and
1301	  by the matrix cocycle identity \cref{lem:gr_bundleCocycle_matrix} the argument equals
1302	  \((X^I_K)^{-1}\), so the composite equals \(\widehat{g}_{IK}^{\,J}\); the bridges make both
1303	  sides isomorphisms between the same pair of sheaves on \(V_{IJK}\).
1304	\end{proof}

1306	\begin{lemma}\leanok
1307	[Triple-overlap multiplicativity of the bundle transition (C2)]
1308	  \label{lem:gr_bundleCocycle_mul}
1309	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.bundleTransition_cocycle}
1310	  \uses{def:gr_bundleTransition, lem:gr_bundleCocycle_matrix, lem:gr_matrixToFreeIso_mul,
1311	    lem:gr_bundleCocycle_transport, lem:modules_pullback_basechange_transport,
1312	    lem:gr_glueData_bridges, def:modules_pullbackComp}
1313	  Over each triple overlap \(V_{IJK} = U^I_J \times_{U^I} U^I_K\) the base-change transports
1314	  (\cref{lem:modules_pullback_basechange_transport}) of the three bundle transitions satisfy
1315	  \[
1316	    \widehat{g}_{JK}^{\,I} \circ \widehat{g}_{IJ}^{\,K}
1317	      \;=\; \widehat{g}_{IK}^{\,J},
1318	  \]
1319	  the module-level form of the descent condition (C2) of \cref{def:scheme_modules_glue} for
1320	  the bundle data \((g_{I,J})\). This is the \(\mathrm{GL}_d\)-valued multiplicative cocycle
1321	  of \(\mathcal{U}\).
1322	\end{lemma}
1323	\begin{proof}
1324	  \uses{lem:gr_bundleCocycle_matrix, lem:gr_matrixToFreeIso_mul,
1325	    lem:gr_bundleCocycle_transport}
1326	  The statement decomposes into three independent steps. First, the matrix-algebra core
1327	  \cref{lem:gr_bundleCocycle_matrix} establishes the Cramer-inverse cocycle
1328	  \((X^J_K)^{-1}(X^I_J)^{-1} = (X^I_K)^{-1}\) over the triple overlap. Second, the linkage
1329	  \cref{lem:gr_matrixToFreeIso_mul} records that composition of matrix automorphisms realises
1330	  matrix multiplication. Third, the transport step \cref{lem:gr_bundleCocycle_transport}
1331	  carries the three transitions to the common overlap \(V_{IJK}\), aligns their source,
1332	  middle, and target sheaves through the base-change transport and the endpoint bridges, and
1333	  -- feeding it the matrix identity through the linkage -- yields exactly the stated equality
1334	  \(\widehat{g}_{JK}^{\,I} \circ \widehat{g}_{IJ}^{\,K} = \widehat{g}_{IK}^{\,J}\).
1335	\end{proof}

1340	[The universal quotient sheaf \(\mathcal{U}\)]
1341	  \label{def:gr_universal_quotient_sheaf}
1342	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.universalQuotient}
1343	  \uses{def:gr_chart_quotient, def:gr_universalMinorInv, def:gr_transition,
1344	    lem:gr_cocycle, def:gr_glued_scheme, def:scheme_modules_glue,
1345	    def:gr_modules_glueRestrictionIso, def:gr_bundleTransition,
1346	    lem:gr_bundleCocycle_id, lem:gr_bundleCocycle_mul,
1347	    lem:gr_glueData_bridges, def:is_locally_free_of_rank}
1348	  % SOURCE: [Nitsure], "Construction of Hilbert and Quot Schemes", \S 1,
1349	  % sub-section "Universal quotient" (read from
1350	  % references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L903-L910).
1351	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
1352	  % "Compatible with the co-cycle $(\theta_{I,J})$
1353	  %  for gluing the affine pieces $U^I$, we give gluing data
1354	  %  $(g_{I,J})$ for gluing together the trivial bundles $\oplus^d\,{\mathcal O}_{U^I}$
1355	  %  by putting
1356	  %  $$g_{I,J} = (X^I_J)^{-1} \in GL_d(U^I_J)$$"
1357	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S 1.}
1358	  Over each overlap \(U^I_J = \Spec R^I_J\) define the bundle-transition matrix
1359	  \[
1360	    g_{I,J} \;=\; (X^I_J)^{-1} \;\in\; \mathrm{GL}_d(R^I_J),
1361	  \]
1362	  the Cramer inverse of the localised \(J\)-minor (\cref{def:gr_universalMinorInv}),
1363	  which is invertible because \(\det(X^I_J)\) is a unit of \(R^I_J\). The matrices
1364	  \((g_{I,J})\) are compatible with the chart-gluing cocycle \((\theta_{I,J})\)
1365	  (\cref{def:gr_transition}, \cref{lem:gr_cocycle}): over a triple overlap they
1366	  satisfy \(g_{I,K} = g_{J,K}\, g_{I,J}\) and \(g_{I,I} = 1\), the multiplicative
1367	  cocycle condition for a vector bundle. Gluing the free rank-\(d\) sheaves
1368	  \(\mathcal{O}_{U^I}^{\,d}\) along the isomorphisms \(g_{I,J}\) over the cover
1369	  \(\{U^I\}\) of \(\mathrm{Gr}(r,d)\) (\cref{def:gr_glued_scheme}) therefore
1370	  produces a locally free \(\mathcal{O}_{\mathrm{Gr}(r,d)}\)-module
1371	  \(\mathcal{U}\) of rank \(d\). Its determinant line bundle \(\det \mathcal{U}\)
1372	  has transition functions \(\det(g_{I,J}) = 1/P^I_J\), the data underlying the
1373	  Pl\"ucker embedding.
1374	\end{definition}

1376	The tautological quotient \(u\) is assembled from the chart quotients \(u^I\) by the
1377	section-level descent \cref{def:gr_modules_glueHom}; its one remaining content is the
1378	\emph{overlap-compatibility} of the family \((u^I)\) with the bundle gluing
1379	\((g_{I,J})\). The next four blocks isolate that compatibility: a rectangular analogue of
1380	the square matrix-endomorphism API (\cref{def:gr_matrixEnd},
1381	\cref{lem:gr_matrixEnd_pullback}, \cref{lem:gr_matrixEnd_comp}) for the
1382	\(r \to d\) chart quotient (\cref{def:gr_matrixEndRect},
1383	\cref{lem:gr_matrixEndRect_pullback}, \cref{lem:gr_matrixEndRect_comp}), the
1384	adjunction transposition that turns the descent condition into a pullback-level identity
1385	(\cref{lem:gr_tautologicalQuotientComponent_transpose}), and the matrix-core assembly
1386	that closes it (\cref{lem:gr_tautologicalQuotient_overlap}).

1390	\leanok
1391	[Rectangular matrix homomorphism of free sheaves]
1392	  \label{def:gr_matrixEndRect}
1393	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.matrixEndRect}
1394	  \uses{def:gr_scalarEnd}
1395	  Let \(S\) be a scheme, \(d, r \in \mathbb{N}\), and
1396	  \(M \in \operatorname{Mat}_{d \times r}(\Gamma(S, \mathcal{O}_S))\) a rectangular matrix
1397	  of global functions. The \emph{rectangular matrix homomorphism}
1398	  \(\mathrm{matrixEndRect}(M)\) is the morphism of free sheaves
1399	  \[
1400	    \mathrm{matrixEndRect}(M) \;:\; \mathcal{O}_S^{\,r}
1401	      \;\longrightarrow\; \mathcal{O}_S^{\,d}
1402	  \]
1403	  whose \((k,\ell)\)-component -- \(k \in \{1,\dots,d\}\) an output index,
1404	  \(\ell \in \{1,\dots,r\}\) an input index -- under the presentation of
1405	  \(\mathcal{O}_S^{\,r}\) and \(\mathcal{O}_S^{\,d}\) as finite biproducts of copies of the
1406	  unit module \(\mathbf 1\), is the scalar endomorphism \(\mathrm{scalarEnd}(M_{k,\ell})\)
1407	  (\cref{def:gr_scalarEnd}). It is assembled from these components by the universal property
1408	  of the two biproducts, exactly as the square \(\mathrm{matrixEnd}\)
1409	  (\cref{def:gr_matrixEnd}) and the chart quotient (\cref{def:gr_chart_quotient}). When
1410	  \(r = d\) it specialises to \(\mathrm{matrixEnd}\); and the chart quotient \(u^I\)
1411	  (\cref{def:gr_chart_quotient}) is by construction \(\mathrm{matrixEndRect}(X^I)\) of the
1412	  universal \(d \times r\) matrix \(X^I\) (\cref{def:gr_universal_matrix}).
1413	\end{definition}

1415	\begin{lemma}
1416	
1417	\leanok
1418	[Rectangular matrix homomorphism is natural under pullback]
1419	  \label{lem:gr_matrixEndRect_pullback}
1420	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.matrixEndRect_pullback}
1421	  \uses{def:gr_matrixEndRect, def:gr_scalarEnd, lem:gr_scalarEnd_pullback,
1422	    lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso, def:modules_pullbackComp, lem:gr_matrixEnd_pullback}
1423	  Let \(p : T \to S\) be a morphism of schemes and
1424	  \(M \in \operatorname{Mat}_{d\times r}(\Gamma(S,\mathcal{O}_S))\), with entrywise
1425	  base-changed matrix \(p^{\sharp}M \in \operatorname{Mat}_{d\times r}(\Gamma(T,\mathcal{O}_T))\).
1426	  Write \(Q_r : p^{*}\mathcal{O}_S^{\,r} \xrightarrow{\sim} \mathcal{O}_T^{\,r}\) and
1427	  \(Q_d : p^{*}\mathcal{O}_S^{\,d} \xrightarrow{\sim} \mathcal{O}_T^{\,d}\) for the
1428	  free-pullback comparisons (\cref{lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso}) at the index sets
1429	  \(\{1,\dots,r\}\) and \(\{1,\dots,d\}\). Then the pullback of
1430	  \(\mathrm{matrixEndRect}(M)\) is conjugate, through \(Q_r\) and \(Q_d\), to the
1431	  rectangular homomorphism of the base-changed matrix:
1432	  \[
1433	    p^{*}\bigl(\mathrm{matrixEndRect}(M)\bigr)
1434	      \;=\; Q_d^{-1} \,\circ\, \mathrm{matrixEndRect}(p^{\sharp}M) \,\circ\, Q_r .
1435	  \]
1436	\end{lemma}
1437	\begin{proof}
1438	  \uses{def:gr_matrixEndRect, lem:gr_scalarEnd_pullback, lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso,
1439	    def:modules_pullbackComp, lem:gr_matrixEnd_pullback}
1440	  Identical to the square case \cref{lem:gr_matrixEnd_pullback}, with the source and target
1441	  biproducts now indexed by \(\{1,\dots,r\}\) and \(\{1,\dots,d\}\) respectively. The
1442	  pullback functor \(p^{*}\), a left adjoint, is additive and biproduct-preserving; through
1443	  the coproduct comparisons underlying the free-pullback isomorphisms
1444	  \cref{lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso} it carries the biproduct presentation of
1445	  \(\mathcal{O}_S^{\,r}\) (resp. \(\mathcal{O}_S^{\,d}\)) to that of \(\mathcal{O}_T^{\,r}\)
1446	  (resp. \(\mathcal{O}_T^{\,d}\)), the reassociation of the iterated pullbacks being
1447	  \cref{def:modules_pullbackComp}. On each single \((k,\ell)\)-component the pulled-back
1448	  scalar endomorphism is, by \cref{lem:gr_scalarEnd_pullback}, the comparison-conjugate of
1449	  \(\mathrm{scalarEnd}((p^{\sharp}M)_{k,\ell})\). Reassembling over the two biproducts, the
1450	  conjugating one-element comparisons combine into \(Q_r\) on the source and \(Q_d\) on the
1451	  target, giving \(p^{*}(\mathrm{matrixEndRect}(M)) = Q_d^{-1}\circ
1452	  \mathrm{matrixEndRect}(p^{\sharp}M)\circ Q_r\).
1453	\end{proof}

1455	\begin{lemma}
1456	
1457	\leanok
1458	[Square-after-rectangular composition law]
1459	  \label{lem:gr_matrixEndRect_comp}
1460	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.matrixEndRect_comp}
1461	  \uses{def:gr_matrixEndRect, def:gr_matrixEnd, lem:gr_scalarEnd_comp, lem:gr_scalarEnd_add,
1462	    lem:gr_matrixEnd_comp}
1463	  For \(M \in \operatorname{Mat}_{d\times r}(\Gamma(S,\mathcal{O}_S))\) and
1464	  \(N \in \operatorname{Mat}_{d\times d}(\Gamma(S,\mathcal{O}_S))\), post-composing the
1465	  rectangular homomorphism with a square one realises the matrix product, with the order
1466	  reversed by the contravariance of the component indexing exactly as in
1467	  \cref{lem:gr_matrixEnd_comp}:
1468	  \[
1469	    \mathrm{matrixEndRect}(M) \,\circ\, \mathrm{matrixEnd}(N)
1470	      \;=\; \mathrm{matrixEndRect}(N \cdot M),
1471	  \]
1472	  the composite \(\mathcal{O}_S^{\,r} \xrightarrow{\mathrm{matrixEndRect}(M)}
1473	  \mathcal{O}_S^{\,d} \xrightarrow{\mathrm{matrixEnd}(N)} \mathcal{O}_S^{\,d}\) (here
1474	  \(N \cdot M\) is the \(d \times r\) product).

1481	  than \(\{1,\dots,d\}\). The composite of two biproduct-assembled morphisms is again
1482	  biproduct-assembled, with \((k,\ell)\)-component the sum over the middle index \(m\) of the
1483	  composites of components, \(\sum_m \mathrm{scalarEnd}(N_{k,m}) \circ
1484	  \mathrm{scalarEnd}(M_{m,\ell})\). Each composite of scalar endomorphisms is the scalar
1485	  endomorphism of the product (\cref{lem:gr_scalarEnd_comp}) and a finite sum of scalar

1487	  that component is \(\mathrm{scalarEnd}(\sum_m N_{k,m} M_{m,\ell}) =
1488	  \mathrm{scalarEnd}((N\cdot M)_{k,\ell})\), the \((k,\ell)\)-component of
1489	  \(\mathrm{matrixEndRect}(N\cdot M)\).

1492	\begin{lemma}
1493	
1494	\leanok
1495	[Adjunction transpose of the chart-overlap condition]
1496	  \label{lem:gr_tautologicalQuotientComponent_transpose}

1498	  \uses{def:gr_chart_quotient, def:gr_bundleTransition, def:gr_modules_glueHom,

1500	    lem:conjugateEquiv_pullbackComp_inv_mathlib, lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso,

1502	  Fix size-\(d\) subsets \(I, J\) and let \(f_{IJ} : U^I_J \hookrightarrow U^I\),
1503	  \(t_{IJ} : U^I_J \xrightarrow{\sim} U^J_I\) be the overlap immersion and transition
1504	  (\cref{def:gr_transition}). The per-chart components \(u^I\) of the tautological quotient
1505	  are the adjoint transposes, along the chart immersions, of the chart quotients
1506	  \cref{def:gr_chart_quotient} (precomposed with the free-pullback comparison). Their
1507	  descent compatibility -- the hypothesis the gluing primitive \cref{def:gr_modules_glueHom}
1508	  consumes, namely that the bundle transition \(g_{I,J}\) (\cref{def:gr_bundleTransition})
1509	  intertwines the pullbacks of \(u^I\) and \(u^J\) over \(U^I_J\) -- is equivalent, under the
1510	  pullback--pushforward adjunction of the overlap immersion, to the pullback-level identity
1511	  of morphisms \(\mathcal{O}_{U^I_J}^{\,r} \to \mathcal{O}_{U^I_J}^{\,d}\)
1512	  \[
1513	    g_{I,J} \,\circ\, f_{IJ}^{*}\,u^I
1514	      \;=\; (t_{IJ} \circ f_{JI})^{*}\,u^J,
