1516	  understood through the free-pullback comparisons (\cref{lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso}) on
1517	  source and target.
1518	\end{lemma}
1519	\begin{proof}
1520	  \uses{lem:gr_homEquiv_conjugateEquiv_app, lem:conjugateEquiv_pullbackComp_inv_mathlib,
1521	    lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso, def:modules_pullbackComp}
1522	  The components are produced by applying the adjunction hom-equivalence to the chart
1523	  quotients; the descent condition of \cref{def:gr_modules_glueHom} is stated on those
1524	  transposes. Transposing back across the adjunction is the naturality of the conjugate
1525	  (mate) construction against the hom-equivalences, \cref{lem:gr_homEquiv_conjugateEquiv_app},
1526	  together with the compatibility of the conjugate with the iterated-pullback comparison
1527	  \cref{lem:conjugateEquiv_pullbackComp_inv_mathlib} reassociating the two pullbacks
1528	  \(f_{IJ}^{*}\) and \((t_{IJ}\circ f_{JI})^{*}\) via \cref{def:modules_pullbackComp}. The
1529	  free-pullback comparisons \cref{lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso} re-present both sides as morphisms
1530	  of the trivial bundles \(\mathcal{O}_{U^I_J}^{\,r} \to \mathcal{O}_{U^I_J}^{\,d}\), under
1531	  which the transposed condition becomes the displayed pullback-level identity.
1532	\end{proof}
1533	
1534	\begin{lemma}
1535	
1536	\leanok
1537	[Overlap compatibility of the tautological quotient]
1538	  \label{lem:gr_tautologicalQuotient_overlap}
1539	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.tautologicalQuotient_overlap}
1540	  \uses{lem:gr_tautologicalQuotientComponent_transpose, lem:gr_matrixEndRect_pullback,
1541	    lem:gr_matrixEndRect_comp, def:gr_bundleTransition, def:gr_chart_quotient,
1542	    def:gr_universalMinorInv, lem:gr_universalMatrix_map_transitionPreMap,
1543	    lem:gr_imageMatrix_map_eq, lem:modules_pullback_basechange_transport,
1544	    lem:gr_baseChange_bridge_left}
1545	  For every pair of size-\(d\) subsets \(I, J\), the chart quotients \(u^I\)
1546	  (\cref{def:gr_chart_quotient}) satisfy the overlap-compatibility condition required by the
1547	  section-level descent \cref{def:gr_modules_glueHom} for the bundle gluing \((g_{I,J})\):
1548	  the pullback-level identity \(g_{I,J} \circ f_{IJ}^{*} u^I = (t_{IJ}\circ f_{JI})^{*} u^J\)
1549	  of \cref{lem:gr_tautologicalQuotientComponent_transpose} holds on each overlap \(U^I_J\).
1550	\end{lemma}
1551	\begin{proof}
1552	  \uses{lem:gr_tautologicalQuotientComponent_transpose, lem:gr_matrixEndRect_pullback,
1553	    lem:gr_matrixEndRect_comp, def:gr_bundleTransition,
1554	    lem:gr_universalMatrix_map_transitionPreMap, lem:gr_imageMatrix_map_eq,
1555	    lem:gr_baseChange_bridge_left}
1556	  By \cref{lem:gr_tautologicalQuotientComponent_transpose} it suffices to prove the
1557	  pullback-level identity \(g_{I,J} \circ f_{IJ}^{*} u^I = (t_{IJ}\circ f_{JI})^{*} u^J\) on
1558	  \(U^I_J\). Both sides are morphisms of trivial bundles \(\mathcal{O}_{U^I_J}^{\,r} \to
1559	  \mathcal{O}_{U^I_J}^{\,d}\); write each as \(\mathrm{matrixEndRect}\) of a \(d \times r\)
1560	  matrix over the overlap ring \(R^I_J\) and compare matrices.
1561	
1562	  Since \(u^I = \mathrm{matrixEndRect}(X^I)\) (\cref{def:gr_chart_quotient}), the
1563	  base-change naturality \cref{lem:gr_matrixEndRect_pullback} identifies \(f_{IJ}^{*} u^I\),
1564	  through the free-pullback comparisons, with \(\mathrm{matrixEndRect}\) of the localised
1565	  matrix \(X^I\) over \(R^I_J\) (the global-sections base change is the away-localisation
1566	  \cref{lem:gr_baseChange_bridge_left}). The bundle transition \(g_{I,J}\) is
1567	  \(\mathrm{matrixEnd}\) of the Cramer inverse \((X^I_J)^{-1}\)
1568	  (\cref{def:gr_bundleTransition}, \cref{def:gr_universalMinorInv}); hence by the
1569	  square-after-rectangular composition law \cref{lem:gr_matrixEndRect_comp},
1570	  \[
1571	    g_{I,J} \circ f_{IJ}^{*} u^I
1572	      \;=\; \mathrm{matrixEndRect}\bigl((X^I_J)^{-1} \cdot X^I\bigr).
1573	  \]
1574	  The transition image matrix identity \(X^J = (X^I_J)^{-1} X^I\)
1575	  (\cref{lem:gr_universalMatrix_map_transitionPreMap}, transported across the overlap by
1576	  \cref{lem:gr_imageMatrix_map_eq}) turns the right-hand matrix into \(X^J\). Symmetrically,
1577	  \(\mathrm{matrixEndRect}_J\) of \(X^J\) is, by \cref{lem:gr_matrixEndRect_pullback} along
1578	  \(t_{IJ}\circ f_{JI}\), the comparison form of \((t_{IJ}\circ f_{JI})^{*} u^J\). Therefore
1579	  the two sides agree, which is the required overlap compatibility.
1580	\end{proof}

1582	\begin{definition}
1583	
1584	\leanok
1585	[The tautological quotient \(u : \mathcal{O}^{r} \twoheadrightarrow \mathcal{U}\)]
1586	  \label{def:tautological_quotient}
1587	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.tautologicalQuotient}
1588	  \uses{def:gr_universal_quotient_sheaf, def:gr_chart_quotient,
1589	    def:gr_glued_scheme, def:gr_universalMinorInv, lem:gr_cocycle,
1590	    def:scheme_modules_glue, def:gr_modules_glueRestrictionIso,
1591	    def:gr_modules_glueHom, lem:gr_tautologicalQuotient_overlap}
1592	  % SOURCE: [Nitsure], "Construction of Hilbert and Quot Schemes", \S 1,
1593	  % sub-section "Universal quotient" (read from
1594	  % references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L908-L910).
1595	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
1596	  % "This is compatible with the homomorphisms $u^I$,
1597	  %  so we get a surjective homomorphism
1598	  %  $u : \oplus^r\,{\mathcal O}_{\grass(r,d)} \to \U$."
1599	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S 1.}
1600	  The chart quotients \(u^I : \mathcal{O}_{U^I}^{\,r} \to \mathcal{O}_{U^I}^{\,d}\)
1601	  (\cref{def:gr_chart_quotient}) are compatible with the bundle gluing
1602	  \((g_{I,J})\) of \(\mathcal{U}\) (\cref{def:gr_universal_quotient_sheaf}): over
1603	  each overlap \(U^I_J\) one has the identity of \(d \times r\) matrices
1604	  \[
1605	    g_{I,J}\, X^I \;=\; (X^I_J)^{-1} X^I \;=\; X^J \qquad \text{on } U^I_J,
1606	  \]
1607	  which is precisely the defining formula of the chart transition
1608	  \(\theta_{I,J}\) (\cref{def:gr_transition}, \cref{def:gr_universalMinorInv}). At the
1609	  module level this overlap compatibility -- the equalising condition consumed by the
1610	  section-level descent \cref{def:gr_modules_glueHom} -- is exactly
1611	  \cref{lem:gr_tautologicalQuotient_overlap}, whose proof routes the matrix identity above
1612	  through the rectangular pullback/composition API
1613	  (\cref{lem:gr_matrixEndRect_pullback}, \cref{lem:gr_matrixEndRect_comp}) and the
1614	  adjunction transpose \cref{lem:gr_tautologicalQuotientComponent_transpose}.
1615	  Hence the local maps \(u^I\), transported through the trivialisations of
1616	  \(\mathcal{U}\), agree on overlaps and glue to a single surjective
1617	  \(\mathcal{O}_{\mathrm{Gr}(r,d)}\)-linear homomorphism
1618	  \[
1619	    u \;:\; \mathcal{O}_{\mathrm{Gr}(r,d)}^{\,r}
1620	      \;\twoheadrightarrow\; \mathcal{U},
1621	  \]
1622	  the \emph{tautological} (or \emph{universal}) rank-\(d\) quotient of the trivial
1623	  rank-\(r\) sheaf. It is surjective because each \(u^I\) is (split) surjective and
1624	  surjectivity is local on \(\mathrm{Gr}(r,d)\), and \(\mathcal{U}\) is locally
1625	  free of rank \(d\) by \cref{def:gr_universal_quotient_sheaf}. The pair
1626	  \(\langle \mathcal{U}, u \rangle\) is the family of rank-\(d\) quotients carried
1627	  by \(\mathrm{Gr}(r,d)\) itself.
1628	\end{definition}

1630	\section{The functor of points}
1631	\label{sec:grquot_functor}
1632	
1633	The Grassmannian functor sends a scheme \(T\) to the set of \emph{equivalence classes} of
1634	rank-\(d\) quotients of \(\mathcal{O}_T^{\,r}\). The following block packages a single such
1635	quotient, the equivalence relation on them, and the pullback action through which the
1636	functor acts on morphisms; the two coherence blocks after it isolate the comparison
1637	identities that make that action functorial.
1638	
1639	\begin{definition}[Rank-\(d\) quotients and their pullback action]
1640	  \label{def:gr_rankQuotient}
1641	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.RankQuotient,
1642	        AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.RankQuotient.Rel,
1643	        AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.RankQuotient.rel_refl,
1644	        AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.RankQuotient.rel_symm,
1645	        AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.RankQuotient.rel_trans,
1646	        AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.rqSetoid,
1647	        AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.rqPullback,
1648	        AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.rqPullback_rel}
1649	  \uses{lem:gr_pullback_isLocallyFreeOfRank, lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso,
1650	        def:is_locally_free_of_rank}
1651	  A \emph{rank-\(d\) quotient of \(\mathcal{O}_T^{\,r}\)} on a scheme \(T\) is a triple
1652	  \(x = \langle \mathcal{F}, q \rangle\) consisting of a sheaf of \(\mathcal{O}_T\)-modules
1653	  \(\mathcal{F}\) that is locally free of rank \(d\) (\cref{def:is_locally_free_of_rank})
1654	  together with an epimorphism \(q : \mathcal{O}_T^{\,r} \twoheadrightarrow \mathcal{F}\) out
1655	  of the trivial rank-\(r\) bundle. Two such quotients \(x = \langle \mathcal{F}, q\rangle\)
1656	  and \(y = \langle \mathcal{F}', q'\rangle\) are \emph{equivalent}, written \(x \sim y\),
1657	  when there is an isomorphism \(f : \mathcal{F} \xrightarrow{\sim} \mathcal{F}'\) with
1658	  \(q \circ f = q'\) (equivalently \(\ker q = \ker q'\)). This relation is reflexive
1659	  (\(f = \mathrm{id}\)), symmetric (\(f \mapsto f^{-1}\)), and transitive (composition of the
1660	  witnessing isomorphisms), hence an equivalence relation -- the setoid whose quotient is the
1661	  functor's value at \(T\).
1662	
1663	  For a morphism \(\psi : T' \to T\) the \emph{pullback action} sends
1664	  \(x = \langle \mathcal{F}, q\rangle\) to
1665	  \[
1666	    \psi^{*}x \;=\;
1667	      \bigl\langle\, \psi^{*}\mathcal{F},\;
1668	        \mathcal{O}_{T'}^{\,r} \xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }
1669	        \psi^{*}\bigl(\mathcal{O}_T^{\,r}\bigr) \xrightarrow{\ \psi^{*}q\ }
1670	        \psi^{*}\mathcal{F} \,\bigr\rangle,
1671	  \]
1672	  re-presenting the source as the trivial bundle via the free-pullback isomorphism
1673	  \cref{lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso}. The result is again a rank-\(d\) quotient: the composite is
1674	  an epimorphism because pullback, a left adjoint, preserves epimorphisms and the
1675	  re-presentation is an isomorphism, and \(\psi^{*}\mathcal{F}\) is locally free of rank \(d\)
1676	  by \cref{lem:gr_pullback_isLocallyFreeOfRank}. The pullback action respects the equivalence
1677	  relation -- given \(f\) witnessing \(x \sim y\), the pulled-back isomorphism \(\psi^{*}f\)
1678	  witnesses \(\psi^{*}x \sim \psi^{*}y\), by functoriality of pullback -- so it descends to a
1679	  map of equivalence classes.
1680	\end{definition}
1681	
1682	The two blocks below record the comparison identities, between the free-pullback isomorphism
1683	\cref{lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso} and Mathlib's pseudofunctor structure on pullback, that make
1684	the descended pullback action of \cref{def:gr_rankQuotient} functorial.
1685	
1686	\begin{lemma}\leanok
1687	[Identity coherence of the free-pullback comparison]
1688	  \label{lem:gr_pullbackObjUnitToUnit_id}
1689	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit_id}
1690	  \uses{lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso}
1691	  For the identity morphism \(\mathrm{id}_T\), the canonical comparison morphism of unit
1692	  modules \(\mathrm{id}_T^{*}\mathbf{1} \to \mathbf{1}\) -- the map underlying the
1693	  free-pullback isomorphism \cref{lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso} at a one-element index -- coincides
1694	  with the component at \(\mathbf{1}\) of the pullback-identity isomorphism
1695	  \(\mathrm{id}_T^{*} \cong \mathrm{id}\).
1696	\end{lemma}
1697	
1698	\begin{proof}
1699	  \uses{lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso}
1700	  \leanok
1701	  The comparison morphism is the mate of \(\mathrm{id}_{\mathbf 1}\) under the
1702	  pullback--pushforward adjunction: the image of the identity of \(\mathbf 1\) through the
1703	  adjunction's hom-equivalence on the unit. For the identity scheme morphism the pushforward
1704	  is the identity functor, so the adjunction unit is itself an identity; Mathlib's
1705	  pullback-identity isomorphism is exactly the conjugate (left-adjoint transpose) of the
1706	  pushforward-identity isomorphism. Tracing the identity through the hom-equivalence and the
1707	  conjugate-transpose identity for the pullback-identity isomorphism shows the comparison
1708	  equals the \(\mathbf 1\)-component of that isomorphism.
1709	\end{proof}
1710	
1711	\begin{lemma}\leanok
1712	[Identity coherence at the free sheaf]
1713	  \label{lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso_id}
1714	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso_id}
1715	  \uses{lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso, lem:gr_pullbackObjUnitToUnit_id}
1716	  For the identity morphism \(\mathrm{id}_T\) and an index set \(I\), the free-pullback
1717	  isomorphism \(\mathrm{id}_T^{*}(\bigoplus_I \mathbf 1) \cong \bigoplus_I \mathbf 1\) coincides
1718	  with the component at the free sheaf \(\bigoplus_I \mathbf 1\) of the pullback-identity
1719	  isomorphism \(\mathrm{id}_T^{*} \cong \mathrm{id}\).
1720	\end{lemma}
1721	
1722	\begin{proof}
1723	  \uses{lem:gr_pullbackObjUnitToUnit_id}
1724	  \leanok
1725	  Both sides are cocones on the coproduct \(\bigoplus_I \mathbf 1\); by coproduct
1726	  extensionality it suffices to check equality after precomposing with each coprojection
1727	  \(\mathbf 1 \to \bigoplus_I \mathbf 1\), which reduces to the one-element case
1728	  \cref{lem:gr_pullbackObjUnitToUnit_id}.
1729	\end{proof}
1730	
1731	\begin{lemma}\leanok
1732	[Composite coherence of the free-pullback comparison]
1733	  \label{lem:gr_pullbackObjUnitToUnit_comp}
1734	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit_comp}
1735	  \uses{lem:gr_pullbackObjUnitToUnit_id, def:modules_pullbackComp}
1736	  For composable scheme morphisms \(a : T'' \to T'\) and \(b : T' \to T\), the comparison
1737	  morphism of unit modules \((b \circ a)^{*}\mathbf{1} \to \mathbf{1}\) factors as
1738	  \(a^{*}\) of the comparison for \(b\), the comparison for \(a\), and the component at
1739	  \(\mathbf{1}\) of the iterated-pullback comparison \(a^{*}b^{*} \cong (b\circ a)^{*}\)
1740	  (\cref{def:modules_pullbackComp}).
1741	\end{lemma}
1742	
1743	\begin{proof}
1744	  \uses{lem:gr_pullbackObjUnitToUnit_id, def:modules_pullbackComp,
1745	    lem:gr_homEquiv_conjugateEquiv_app}
1746	    \leanok
1747	  This is the composite analogue of \cref{lem:gr_pullbackObjUnitToUnit_id}: the mate
1748	  construction is compatible with composition of adjunctions, so the comparison for
1749	  \(b \circ a\) is the pasting of the comparisons for \(a\) and \(b\) reassociated by the
1750	  pseudofunctor comparison \cref{def:modules_pullbackComp}. The compatibility of the mate
1751	  with the adjunction hom-equivalences is supplied by
1752	  \cref{lem:gr_homEquiv_conjugateEquiv_app}, the reusable conjugate-naturality engine. The
1753	  pentagon coherence of the iterated-pullback comparisons makes the factorisation
1754	  well-defined.
1755	\end{proof}
1756	
1757	\begin{lemma}\leanok
1758	[Mate/conjugate compatibility of the adjunction hom-equivalence]
1759	  \label{lem:gr_homEquiv_conjugateEquiv_app}
1760	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.homEquiv_conjugateEquiv_app}
1761	  Let \(\mathrm{adj}_1, \mathrm{adj}_2\) be two adjunctions and \(\alpha\) a natural
1762	  transformation between their left adjoints. For a morphism \(f\) the hom-equivalence of
1763	  \(\mathrm{adj}_2\) applied to \(\alpha_c \circ f\) equals the hom-equivalence of
1764	  \(\mathrm{adj}_1\) applied to \(f\), post-composed with the component at \(d\) of the
1765	  conjugate (mate) of \(\alpha\). This is the general naturality of the conjugate
1766	  construction against the two hom-equivalences; it is the reusable engine behind
1767	  \cref{lem:gr_pullbackObjUnitToUnit_comp}.
1768	\end{lemma}
1769	
1770	\begin{proof}
1771	
1772	\leanok
1773	  Both sides unfold to the same pasting of unit/counit triangles for the two adjunctions;
1774	  the equality is the standard compatibility of the mate of \(\alpha\) with the
1775	  hom-set bijections.
1776	\end{proof}
1777	
1778	\begin{lemma}\leanok
1779	[Composite coherence at the free sheaf]
1780	  \label{lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso_comp}
1781	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso_comp}
1782	  \uses{lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso, lem:gr_pullbackObjUnitToUnit_comp, def:modules_pullbackComp}
1783	  The composite analogue of \cref{lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso_id}: for composable \(a, b\) and an
1784	  index set \(I\), the free-pullback isomorphism for \(b \circ a\) at \(\bigoplus_I \mathbf 1\)
1785	  factors through \(a^{*}\) of the free-pullback isomorphism for \(b\), the one for \(a\), and
1786	  the iterated-pullback comparison \cref{def:modules_pullbackComp}.
1787	\end{lemma}
1788	
1789	\begin{proof}
1790	  \uses{lem:gr_pullbackObjUnitToUnit_comp}
1791	  \leanok
1792	  By coproduct extensionality it suffices to check the identity after precomposing with each
1793	  coprojection \(\mathbf 1 \to \bigoplus_I \mathbf 1\), which reduces to the one-element
1794	  composite coherence \cref{lem:gr_pullbackObjUnitToUnit_comp} (with the base-ring sheaf
1795	  tracked through each pullback).
1796	\end{proof}
1797	
1798	Together \cref{lem:gr_pullbackObjUnitToUnit_id} and \cref{lem:gr_pullbackObjUnitToUnit_comp}
1799	upgrade to the corresponding free-sheaf coherences -- the free-pullback isomorphism at the
1800	identity equals the component of the pullback-identity isomorphism, and likewise for the
1801	composite -- by coproduct extensionality over the decomposition
1802	\(\mathcal{O}^{\,\oplus I} = \bigoplus_I \mathbf 1\). These coherences discharge both
1803	functoriality laws (identity and composition) of the Grassmannian
1804	functor \cref{def:grassmannian_functor}.
1805	
1806	\begin{definition}
1807	
1808	\leanok
1809	[The Grassmannian / Quot-of-trivial-sheaf functor \(\operatorname{Grass}(r,d)\)]
1810	  \label{def:grassmannian_functor}
1811	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.functor}
1812	  \uses{def:is_locally_free_of_rank, def:quot_functor, def:gr_rankQuotient,
1813	    lem:gr_pullbackObjUnitToUnit_id, lem:gr_pullbackObjUnitToUnit_comp}
1814	  % SOURCE: [Nitsure], "Construction of Hilbert and Quot Schemes", \S 1,
1815	  % Exercise (2) "Grassmannian as a Quot scheme" (read from
1816	  % references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L557-L564).
1817	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
1818	  % "represents the
1819	  %  contravariant functor
1820	  %  $$\Grass(r,d) = \Quot_{\oplus^r\,{\mathcal O}_{\Z}/\Z/\Z}^{d, {\mathcal O}_{\Z}}$$
1821	  %  from schemes to sets, which associates to any $T$ the
1822	  %  set of all equivalence classes
1823	  %  $\langle \F, q\rangle$ of quotients $q: \oplus^r\,{\mathcal O}_T \to \F$
1824	  %  where $\F$ is a locally free sheaf on $T$ of rank $d$."
1825	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S 1, Exercise (2).}
1826	  The \emph{Grassmannian functor}
1827	  \(\operatorname{Grass}(r,d) = \Quot^{\,d,\,\mathcal{O}_{\mathbb{Z}}}_{\oplus^{r}
1828	  \mathcal{O}_{\mathbb{Z}}/\mathbb{Z}/\mathbb{Z}}\) is the contravariant functor
1829	  from schemes to sets defined as follows. For a scheme \(T\), a \emph{family of
1830	  rank-\(d\) quotients of \(\mathcal{O}_T^{\,r}\)} is a pair
1831	  \(\langle \mathcal{F}, q \rangle\) consisting of a locally free
1832	  \(\mathcal{O}_T\)-module \(\mathcal{F}\) of rank \(d\) together with a surjective
1833	  \(\mathcal{O}_T\)-linear homomorphism \(q : \mathcal{O}_T^{\,r}
1834	  \twoheadrightarrow \mathcal{F}\). Two such families
1835	  \(\langle \mathcal{F}, q \rangle\) and \(\langle \mathcal{F}', q' \rangle\) are
1836	  \emph{equivalent} if there is an isomorphism \(f : \mathcal{F}
1837	  \xrightarrow{\sim} \mathcal{F}'\) with \(f \circ q = q'\); equivalently
1838	  \(\ker(q) = \ker(q')\) as subsheaves of \(\mathcal{O}_T^{\,r}\). Set
1839	  \[
1840	    \operatorname{Grass}(r,d)(T)
1841	      \;=\; \bigl\{\, \text{equivalence classes } \langle \mathcal{F}, q \rangle
1842	        \text{ of rank-}d\text{ quotients of } \mathcal{O}_T^{\,r} \,\bigr\}.
1843	  \]
1844	  For a morphism \(\varphi : T' \to T\), the map
1845	  \(\operatorname{Grass}(r,d)(\varphi)\) sends \(\langle \mathcal{F}, q \rangle\)
1846	  to the pulled-back quotient \(\langle \varphi^{*}\mathcal{F},\, \varphi^{*}q
1847	  \rangle\); this is well-defined on equivalence classes because pullback is right
1848	  exact and preserves local freeness of rank \(d\). (As \(\mathcal{F}\) is locally
1849	  free of rank \(d\), surjectivity is preserved and the support is automatically
1850	  proper -- all of \(T'\) -- so this is the special case
1851	  \(E = \mathcal{O}^{r}\), \(X = S = \operatorname{base}\) of the general Quot
1852	  functor of \cref{def:quot_functor}.)
1853	\end{definition}
1854	
1855	\section{The universal property}
1856	\label{sec:grquot_universal}
1857	
1858	\begin{theorem}
1859	
1860	\leanok
1861	[\(\mathrm{Gr}(r,d)\) represents the Grassmannian functor]
1862	  \label{thm:grassmannian_universal_property}
1863	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.represents}
1864	  \uses{def:grassmannian_functor, def:tautological_quotient,
1865	    def:gr_universal_quotient_sheaf, def:gr_glued_scheme, def:gr_affine_chart,
1866	    def:gr_universal_matrix, def:gr_transition, lem:gr_cocycle}
1867	  % SOURCE: [Nitsure], "Construction of Hilbert and Quot Schemes", \S 1,
1868	  % Exercise (2) "Grassmannian as a Quot scheme" (read from
1869	  % references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L546-L566).
1870	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
1871	  % "Show that $\grass(r,d)$ together with the quotient
1872	  %  $u:\oplus^r\,{\mathcal O}_{\grass(r,d)} \to \U$ represents the
1873	  %  contravariant functor
1874	  %  $$\Grass(r,d) = \Quot_{\oplus^r\,{\mathcal O}_{\Z}/\Z/\Z}^{d, {\mathcal O}_{\Z}}$$
1875	  %  from schemes to sets, which associates to any $T$ the
1876	  %  set of all equivalence classes
1877	  %  $\langle \F, q\rangle$ of quotients $q: \oplus^r\,{\mathcal O}_T \to \F$
1878	  %  where $\F$ is a locally free sheaf on $T$ of rank $d$.
1879	  %  Therefore, $\quot_{\oplus^r\,{\mathcal O}_{\Z}/\Z/\Z}^{d, {\mathcal O}_{\Z}}$
1880	  %  exists, and equals $\grass(r,d)$."
1881	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S 1, Exercise (2).}
1882	  The Grassmannian scheme \(\mathrm{Gr}(r,d)\) (\cref{def:gr_glued_scheme}),
1883	  together with the tautological quotient \(\langle \mathcal{U}, u \rangle\)
1884	  (\cref{def:tautological_quotient}), represents the functor
1885	  \(\operatorname{Grass}(r,d)\) (\cref{def:grassmannian_functor}). That is, for
1886	  every scheme \(T\) the natural map
1887	  \[
1888	    \operatorname{Hom}(T, \mathrm{Gr}(r,d))
1889	      \;\xrightarrow{\;\sim\;}\;
1890	    \operatorname{Grass}(r,d)(T),
1891	    \qquad
1892	    \bigl(\,\varphi : T \to \mathrm{Gr}(r,d)\,\bigr)
1893	      \;\longmapsto\;
1894	    \langle \varphi^{*}\mathcal{U},\, \varphi^{*}u \rangle,
1895	  \]
1896	  is a bijection, natural in \(T\). Consequently the Quot scheme
1897	  \(\quot^{\,d,\,\mathcal{O}_{\mathbb{Z}}}_{\oplus^{r}\mathcal{O}_{\mathbb{Z}}/
1898	  \mathbb{Z}/\mathbb{Z}}\) of rank-\(d\) quotients of the trivial rank-\(r\) sheaf
1899	  exists and equals \(\mathrm{Gr}(r,d)\); the tautological quotient is the
1900	  universal family.
1901	\end{theorem}
1902	
1903	\begin{proof}
1904	  \uses{def:tautological_quotient, def:gr_universal_quotient_sheaf,
1905	    def:gr_chart_quotient, def:gr_affine_chart, def:gr_universal_matrix,
1906	    def:gr_transition, lem:gr_cocycle, def:gr_glued_scheme,
1907	    def:grassmannian_functor}
1908	  Naturality of \(\varphi \mapsto \langle \varphi^{*}\mathcal{U}, \varphi^{*}u
1909	  \rangle\) is immediate: pullback is functorial and \(\varphi^{*}u\) is again a
1910	  surjection onto a rank-\(d\) locally free sheaf, so the assignment defines a
1911	  morphism of functors \(\operatorname{Hom}(-,\mathrm{Gr}(r,d)) \to
1912	  \operatorname{Grass}(r,d)\). It remains to show it is a bijection for each
1913	  \(T\); we exhibit an inverse, constructed Zariski-locally on \(T\) and glued.
1914	
1915	  \emph{From a quotient to a morphism.} Let \(\langle \mathcal{F}, q \rangle \in
1916	  \operatorname{Grass}(r,d)(T)\), so \(q : \mathcal{O}_T^{\,r} \twoheadrightarrow
1917	  \mathcal{F}\) with \(\mathcal{F}\) locally free of rank \(d\). For a size-\(d\)
1918	  subset \(I \subseteq \{1,\dots,r\}\) let \(T_I \subseteq T\) be the locus where
1919	  the composite
1920	  \[
1921	    \mathcal{O}_T^{\,d}
1922	      \overset{\ \iota_I\ }{\hookrightarrow} \mathcal{O}_T^{\,r}
1923	      \xrightarrow{\ q\ } \mathcal{F},
1924	  \]
1925	  with \(\iota_I\) the inclusion of the \(I\)-indexed coordinates, is an
1926	  isomorphism. This locus is open: in a local trivialisation of \(\mathcal{F}\)
1927	  the composite is a \(d \times d\) matrix and \(T_I\) is the non-vanishing locus
1928	  of its determinant. The \(T_I\) cover \(T\): at any point \(t\), the
1929	  \(d\)-dimensional fibre quotient \(q(t) : \kappa(t)^{r} \twoheadrightarrow
1930	  \mathcal{F}\otimes\kappa(t)\) is surjective, so some \(d\) of the \(r\) standard
1931	  basis vectors map to a basis of the \(d\)-dimensional fibre, i.e.\ \(t \in T_I\)
1932	  for that \(I\) (by Nakayama the isomorphism then holds in a neighbourhood).
1933	
1934	  On \(T_I\), trivialise \(\mathcal{F}\) using \(\iota_I^{*}\): the quotient
1935	  becomes a surjection \(q_I : \mathcal{O}_{T_I}^{\,r} \to \mathcal{O}_{T_I}^{\,d}\)
1936	  represented by a \(d \times r\) matrix \(M^I\) whose \(I\)-th minor \(M^I_I\) is
1937	  the identity \(1_{d \times d}\) -- exactly the shape of the universal matrix
1938	  \(X^I\) (\cref{def:gr_universal_matrix}). The \(d(r-d)\) free entries of \(M^I\)
1939	  are global functions on \(T_I\) and hence determine a unique
1940	  \(\mathbb{Z}\)-algebra homomorphism \(R^I = \mathbb{Z}[X^I] \to
1941	  \Gamma(T_I, \mathcal{O}_{T_I})\) sending \(x^I_{p,q}\) to the corresponding entry
1942	  of \(M^I\), i.e.\ a unique morphism \(\varphi_I : T_I \to U^I\)
1943	  (\cref{def:gr_affine_chart}) with \(\varphi_I^{*}X^I = M^I\) and thus
1944	  \(\varphi_I^{*}u^I = q_I\) (\cref{def:gr_chart_quotient}).
1945	
1946	  \emph{Gluing.} On the overlap \(T_I \cap T_J\) the two trivialisations differ by
1947	  the change-of-basis \((M^I_J)^{-1}\), which is exactly the bundle transition
1948	  \(g_{I,J} = (X^I_J)^{-1}\) of \(\mathcal{U}\)
1949	  (\cref{def:gr_universal_quotient_sheaf}); concretely
1950	  \(M^J = (M^I_J)^{-1} M^I = \varphi_I^{*}\theta_{I,J}(X^J)\)
1951	  (\cref{def:gr_transition}, \cref{def:gr_universalMinorInv}). Hence
1952	  \(\varphi_I\) and \(\varphi_J\) land in the overlap charts and agree there after
1953	  the transition \(\theta_{I,J}\), so by the cocycle condition
1954	  (\cref{lem:gr_cocycle}) the \(\varphi_I\) glue to a single morphism
1955	  \(\varphi : T \to \mathrm{Gr}(r,d)\) (\cref{def:gr_glued_scheme}). By
1956	  construction \(\varphi^{*}\langle \mathcal{U}, u \rangle = \langle \mathcal{F},
1957	  q \rangle\), since this holds on each \(T_I\) and the equivalence class is
1958	  determined by \(\ker(q)\).
1959	
1960	  \emph{Uniqueness.} If \(\psi : T \to \mathrm{Gr}(r,d)\) also satisfies
1961	  \(\psi^{*}\langle \mathcal{U}, u \rangle = \langle \mathcal{F}, q \rangle\), then
1962	  on \(\psi^{-1}(U^I)\) the pulled-back tautological quotient \(\psi^{*}u^I\)
1963	  equals \(q\) in the \(I\)-trivialisation, which forces \(\psi^{*}X^I = M^I\) and
1964	  hence \(\psi = \varphi_I\) there; as the \(U^I\) cover \(\mathrm{Gr}(r,d)\),
1965	  \(\psi = \varphi\). Thus \(\varphi\) is the unique morphism inducing
1966	  \(\langle \mathcal{F}, q \rangle\), and \(\varphi \mapsto \langle
1967	  \varphi^{*}\mathcal{U}, \varphi^{*}u \rangle\) is bijective.
1968	
1969	  Therefore \(\mathrm{Gr}(r,d)\) with \(\langle \mathcal{U}, u \rangle\) is a fine
1970	  moduli space: it represents \(\operatorname{Grass}(r,d)\), so
1971	  \(\quot^{\,d,\,\mathcal{O}_{\mathbb{Z}}}_{\oplus^{r}\mathcal{O}_{\mathbb{Z}}/
1972	  \mathbb{Z}/\mathbb{Z}}\) exists and equals \(\mathrm{Gr}(r,d)\).
1973	\end{proof}

802	the matrix endomorphism of the base-changed matrix `p.appTop • M` (entrywise comorphism):
803	`(pullback p).map (matrixEnd M) = Q.hom ≫ matrixEnd (p.appTop.mapMatrix M) ≫ Q.inv`.
804	Reduces, on each one-element biproduct component, to the scalar atom `scalarEnd_pullback`.
805	Project-local. -/
806	lemma matrixEnd_pullback {T S : Scheme.{0}} (p : T ⟶ S) {d : ℕ}
807	    (M : Matrix (Fin d) (Fin d) Γ(S, ⊤)) :
808	    (Scheme.Modules.pullback p).map (matrixEnd M)
809	      = (Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso p (Fin d)).hom ≫
810	        matrixEnd ((CommRingCat.Hom.hom (Scheme.Hom.appTop p)).mapMatrix M) ≫
811	        (Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso p (Fin d)).inv := by
812	  haveI := Scheme.Modules.opensMap_final p
813	  -- Reduce to the naturality square (cancel the trailing `Q.inv`).
814	  rw [← Category.assoc, Iso.eq_comp_inv]
815	  -- Check the two maps out of the coproduct `(pullback p).obj (free (Fin d))` agree on each
816	  -- free injection `(pullback p).map (ιFree i)` (the cofan of the preserved colimit).
817	  refine Cofan.IsColimit.hom_ext
818	    (isColimitCofanMkObjOfIsColimit (Scheme.Modules.pullback p) _ _
819	      (SheafOfModules.isColimitFreeCofan (Fin d))) _ _ (fun i => ?_)
820	  simp only [cofan_mk_inj, Cofan.mk_pt]
821	  -- `Q.hom` is, by construction of `pullbackFreeIso`, the Mathlib free-pullback comparison.
822	  have hQhom : (Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso p (Fin d)).hom
823	      = (SheafOfModules.pullbackObjFreeIso (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom p) (Fin d)).hom := rfl
824	  -- The free injection cancels against `Q.hom` into the unit-pullback comparison
825	  -- (`pullbackObjUnitToUnit`), which is where `scalarEnd_pullback` lives.
826	  have key : ∀ k : Fin d,
827	      (Scheme.Modules.pullback p).map (SheafOfModules.ιFree k)
828	          ≫ (Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso p (Fin d)).hom
829	        = SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom p)
830	            ≫ SheafOfModules.ιFree k := by
831	    intro k
832	    rw [hQhom]
833	    exact SheafOfModules.pullback_map_ιFree_comp_pullbackObjFreeIso_hom _ k
834	  -- LHS: `map (ιFree i) ≫ map (matrixEnd M)` collapses to `map (ιFree i ≫ matrixEnd M)`,
835	  -- then `ιFree_matrixEnd` turns it into a row sum, distributed by additivity of the pullback.
836	  rw [← Category.assoc ((Scheme.Modules.pullback p).map (SheafOfModules.ιFree i))
837	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback p).map (matrixEnd M)),
838	    ← Functor.map_comp]
839	  -- `erw` (defeq matching) is needed to see `ιFree i ≫ matrixEnd M` under `(pullback p).map`.
840	  erw [ιFree_matrixEnd M i]
841	  erw [Functor.map_sum]
842	  rw [Preadditive.sum_comp]
843	  -- RHS: cancel `map (ιFree i) ≫ Q.hom` into `pullbackObjUnitToUnit ≫ ιFree i`, then expand.
844	  rw [← Category.assoc ((Scheme.Modules.pullback p).map (SheafOfModules.ιFree i))
845	        (Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso p (Fin d)).hom,
846	    key i]
847	  erw [Category.assoc]
848	  erw [ιFree_matrixEnd ((CommRingCat.Hom.hom (Scheme.Hom.appTop p)).mapMatrix M) i]
849	  erw [Preadditive.comp_sum]
850	  -- Match term by term: each entry reduces to the scalar atom `scalarEnd_pullback`.
851	  refine Finset.sum_congr rfl (fun k _ => ?_)
852	  erw [Functor.map_comp]
853	  rw [Category.assoc, key k]
854	  erw [reassoc_of% scalarEnd_pullback p (M k i)]
855	  erw [Category.assoc]

857	/-- The action of `matrixEndRect M` on the `j`-th free injection: `ιFree j ≫ matrixEndRect M`
858	expands as the sum over rows `∑ k, scalarEnd (M k j) ≫ ιFree k`. Project-local helper
859	for `matrixEndRect_pullback` (rectangular analogue of `ιFree_matrixEnd`). -/
860	lemma ιFree_matrixEndRect {S : Scheme.{0}} {d r : ℕ} (M : Matrix (Fin d) (Fin r) Γ(S, ⊤))
861	    (j : Fin r) :
862	    SheafOfModules.ιFree (R := S.ringCatSheaf) j ≫ matrixEndRect M
863	      = ∑ k, scalarEnd (M k j) ≫ SheafOfModules.ιFree (R := S.ringCatSheaf) k := by
864	  rw [matrixEndRect, SheafOfModules.ιFree]
865	  simp only [SheafOfModules.free]
866	  rw [Iso.symm_hom, biproduct.isoCoproduct_inv, biproduct.isoCoproduct_hom,
867	    ← Category.assoc, Sigma.ι_desc, biproduct.ι_matrix_assoc, biproduct.lift_desc]
868	  rfl

870	/-- **Rectangular matrix homomorphism naturality under pullback**
871	(`lem:gr_matrixEndRect_pullback`). For `p : T ⟶ S` and a `d × r` matrix `M` of global
872	sections, the pullback of `matrixEndRect M` is, after the free-pullback comparisons
873	`Q_r = pullbackFreeIso p (Fin r)` and `Q_d = pullbackFreeIso p (Fin d)`, the rectangular
874	homomorphism of the base-changed matrix `p^♯ M`:
875	`(pullback p).map (matrixEndRect M) = Q_r.hom ≫ matrixEndRect (p^♯ M) ≫ Q_d.inv`.
876	Identical skeleton to the square `matrixEnd_pullback`, reducing on each one-element
877	biproduct component to the scalar atom `scalarEnd_pullback`. Project-local. -/
878	lemma matrixEndRect_pullback {T S : Scheme.{0}} (p : T ⟶ S) {d r : ℕ}
879	    (M : Matrix (Fin d) (Fin r) Γ(S, ⊤)) :
880	    (Scheme.Modules.pullback p).map (matrixEndRect M)
881	      = (Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso p (Fin r)).hom ≫
882	        matrixEndRect (M.map ⇑(CommRingCat.Hom.hom (Scheme.Hom.appTop p))) ≫
883	        (Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso p (Fin d)).inv := by
884	  haveI := Scheme.Modules.opensMap_final p
885	  -- Reduce to the naturality square (cancel the trailing `Q_d.inv`).
886	  rw [← Category.assoc, Iso.eq_comp_inv]
887	  -- Check the two maps out of the coproduct `(pullback p).obj (free (Fin r))` agree on each
888	  -- free injection (the cofan of the preserved colimit).
889	  refine Cofan.IsColimit.hom_ext
890	    (isColimitCofanMkObjOfIsColimit (Scheme.Modules.pullback p) _ _
891	      (SheafOfModules.isColimitFreeCofan (Fin r))) _ _ (fun i => ?_)
892	  simp only [cofan_mk_inj, Cofan.mk_pt]
893	  -- the source/target free-pullback comparisons in their Mathlib form
894	  have hQr : (Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso p (Fin r)).hom
895	      = (SheafOfModules.pullbackObjFreeIso (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom p) (Fin r)).hom := rfl
896	  have hQd : (Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso p (Fin d)).hom
897	      = (SheafOfModules.pullbackObjFreeIso (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom p) (Fin d)).hom := rfl
898	  have key_r : ∀ k : Fin r,
899	      (Scheme.Modules.pullback p).map (SheafOfModules.ιFree k)
900	          ≫ (Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso p (Fin r)).hom
901	        = SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom p)
902	            ≫ SheafOfModules.ιFree k := by
903	    intro k
904	    rw [hQr]
905	    exact SheafOfModules.pullback_map_ιFree_comp_pullbackObjFreeIso_hom _ k
906	  have key_d : ∀ k : Fin d,
907	      (Scheme.Modules.pullback p).map (SheafOfModules.ιFree k)
908	          ≫ (Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso p (Fin d)).hom
909	        = SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom p)
910	            ≫ SheafOfModules.ιFree k := by
911	    intro k
912	    rw [hQd]
913	    exact SheafOfModules.pullback_map_ιFree_comp_pullbackObjFreeIso_hom _ k
914	  -- LHS: collapse to a row sum via `ιFree_matrixEndRect`, distributed by additivity.
915	  rw [← Category.assoc ((Scheme.Modules.pullback p).map (SheafOfModules.ιFree i))
916	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback p).map (matrixEndRect M)),
917	    ← Functor.map_comp]
918	  erw [ιFree_matrixEndRect M i]
919	  erw [Functor.map_sum]
920	  rw [Preadditive.sum_comp]
921	  -- RHS: cancel `map (ιFree i) ≫ Q_r.hom` into `pullbackObjUnitToUnit ≫ ιFree i`, expand.
922	  rw [← Category.assoc ((Scheme.Modules.pullback p).map (SheafOfModules.ιFree i))
923	        (Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso p (Fin r)).hom,
924	    key_r i]
925	  erw [Category.assoc]
926	  erw [ιFree_matrixEndRect (M.map ⇑(CommRingCat.Hom.hom (Scheme.Hom.appTop p))) i]
927	  erw [Preadditive.comp_sum]
928	  -- Match term by term: each entry reduces to the scalar atom `scalarEnd_pullback`.
929	  refine Finset.sum_congr rfl (fun k _ => ?_)
930	  erw [Functor.map_comp]
931	  rw [Category.assoc, key_d k]
932	  erw [reassoc_of% scalarEnd_pullback p (M k i)]
933	  erw [Category.assoc]

948	/-- **Transport of a matrix automorphism through `pullbackBaseChangeTransport`** — the
949	reusable (a)→(c) bridge for the bundle cocycle (`lem:gr_matrixToFreeIso_transport`). A
950	transition isomorphism of the bundle-transition shape
951	`pullbackFreeIso a ≪≫ matrixToFreeIso M N ≪≫ (pullbackFreeIso b).symm` (a `GL_d` matrix
952	automorphism conjugated to the overlap pullbacks) transports along `p : W ⟶ V` to the same
953	shape over `p ≫ a` / `p ≫ b`, with the matrix base-changed entrywise by the comorphism
954	`p.appTop`. Combines the matrix-naturality atom `matrixEnd_pullback` with the free-pullback
955	pseudofunctor coherence `Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso_comp`. Project-local — this is the
956	abstract core of the bundle cocycle transport, independent of the Grassmannian charts. -/
957	lemma pullbackBaseChangeTransport_matrixToFreeIso {W V : Scheme.{0}} (p : W ⟶ V)
958	    {Yi Yj : Scheme.{0}} (a : V ⟶ Yi) (b : V ⟶ Yj) {d : ℕ}
959	    (M N : Matrix (Fin d) (Fin d) Γ(V, ⊤)) (hMN : M * N = 1) (hNM : N * M = 1) :
960	    (Scheme.Modules.pullbackBaseChangeTransport p a b
961	        (Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso a (Fin d) ≪≫ matrixToFreeIso M N hMN hNM ≪≫
962	          (Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso b (Fin d)).symm)).hom
963	      = (Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso (p ≫ a) (Fin d)).hom ≫
964	        matrixEnd ((CommRingCat.Hom.hom (Scheme.Hom.appTop p)).mapMatrix M) ≫
965	        (Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso (p ≫ b) (Fin d)).inv := by
966	  simp only [Scheme.Modules.pullbackBaseChangeTransport, Iso.trans_hom, Functor.mapIso_hom,
967	    Iso.symm_hom, matrixToFreeIso_hom]
968	  -- Front coherence: the `pullbackComp` cast + the `a`-leg comparison assemble into the
969	  -- composite free-pullback comparison `Q_{p≫a}` (pseudofunctoriality, `pullbackFreeIso_comp`).
970	  have hfront : ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp p a).symm.app
971	          (SheafOfModules.free (Fin d))).hom ≫
972	        (Scheme.Modules.pullback p).map (Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso a (Fin d)).hom ≫
973	          (Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso p (Fin d)).hom
974	      = (Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso (p ≫ a) (Fin d)).hom := by
975	    erw [← Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso_comp a p (Fin d)]
976	    simp only [Iso.app_hom, Iso.symm_hom]
977	    rw [Iso.inv_hom_id_app_assoc]
978	  -- Back coherence: the inverse `b`-leg comparison + the `pullbackComp` cast assemble into the
979	  -- inverse composite comparison `Q_{p≫b}⁻¹`. Derived by inverting the `b`-leg coherence iso.
980	  have hback : (Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso p (Fin d)).inv ≫
981	        (Scheme.Modules.pullback p).map (Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso b (Fin d)).inv ≫
982	          ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp p b).app (SheafOfModules.free (Fin d))).hom
983	      = (Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso (p ≫ b) (Fin d)).inv := by
984	    have hiso : (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp p b).app (SheafOfModules.free (Fin d)) ≪≫
985	          Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso (p ≫ b) (Fin d)
986	        = (Scheme.Modules.pullback p).mapIso (Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso b (Fin d)) ≪≫
987	          Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso p (Fin d) := by
988	      apply Iso.ext
989	      simpa using Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso_comp b p (Fin d)
990	    have hinv := congrArg Iso.inv hiso
991	    simp only [Iso.trans_inv, Functor.mapIso_inv, Iso.app_inv] at hinv
992	    -- hinv : Q_{p≫b}.inv ≫ Cpb.inv.app free = Q_p.inv ≫ (pullback p).map Q_b.inv
993	    rw [← Category.assoc, ← hinv, Iso.app_hom]
994	    erw [Category.assoc, Iso.inv_hom_id_app]
995	    rw [Category.comp_id]
996	  -- Distribute `pullback p` over the conjugated matrix automorphism and apply the matrix atom.
997	  rw [Functor.map_comp, Functor.map_comp, matrixEnd_pullback]
998	  -- Expand both comparison legs of the target via the coherences `hfront`/`hback`; the two sides
999	  -- then coincide up to the (here definitional) associativity of the composite.
1000	  rw [← hfront, ← hback]
1001	  rfl

1003	/-! ### The base-change bridge (b): geometric comorphisms = localised cocycle ring homs
1004	
1005	The three scheme-pullback base-change maps `Γ(U^I_J,⊤) ⟶ Γ(V_IJK,⊤)` — induced by the two
1006	pullback projections and the triple transition `t'` — are identified, through the affine
1007	global-sections isomorphism `ΓSpecIso` and the away-pullback identification
1008	`V_IJK ≅ Spec S_I` (`awayPullbackIso`), with the ring homomorphisms `awayInclLeft`,
1009	`awayInclRight` and `cocycleΘIJ ∘ awayInclRight` over which the matrix cocycle L1
1010	(`bundleTransition_cocycle_matrix`) is stated. -/

1012	/-- **Affine global-sections comorphism is the inducing ring homomorphism**
1013	(`lem:gr_baseChange_bridge_gammaSpec`): for a ring homomorphism `φ : A ⟶ B`, the
1014	global-sections comorphism of `Spec.map φ`, conjugated through the counit isomorphisms
1015	`ΓSpecIso`, is `φ` itself. Pure `Γ ⊣ Spec` naturality; project-local packaging. -/
1016	lemma baseChange_bridge_gammaSpec {A B : CommRingCat.{0}} (φ : A ⟶ B) :
1017	    (Scheme.ΓSpecIso A).inv ≫ Scheme.Hom.appTop (Spec.map φ)
1018	      = φ ≫ (Scheme.ΓSpecIso B).inv := by
1019	  rw [Iso.inv_comp_eq, ← Category.assoc, ← Scheme.ΓSpecIso_naturality, Category.assoc,

1819	/-- The **tautological quotient** `u : O^r ↠ U` (`def:tautological_quotient`): the global
1820	surjection assembled from the chart quotients `u^I` (`chartQuotientMap`), compatible with
1821	the bundle gluing of `universalQuotient`. Since `universalQuotient` is the descent
1822	equalizer of pushforwards, the morphism is `glueLift` of the per-chart adjoint
1823	transposes (`tautologicalQuotientComponent`); the equalizing condition — the chart
1824	compatibility `g_{IJ} ∘ f_{IJ}^* u^I = (t_{IJ} ≫ f_{JI})^* u^J`, whose matrix content is
1825	`X^J = (X^I_J)⁻¹ X^I` (`universalMatrix_map_transitionPreMap` / `imageMatrix`) — is
1826	`tautologicalQuotient_overlap`, transposed through the adjunction by
1827	`tautologicalQuotientComponent_transpose`. -/
1828	noncomputable def tautologicalQuotient (d r : ℕ) :
1829	    SheafOfModules.free (R := (scheme d r).ringCatSheaf) (Fin r) ⟶ universalQuotient d r :=
1830	  Scheme.Modules.glueLift (theGlueData d r)
1831	    (fun I => SheafOfModules.free (R := ((theGlueData d r).U I).ringCatSheaf) (Fin d))
1832	    (bundleTransitionData d r)
1833	    (fun I => bundleTransition_self d r I.1 I.2)
1834	    (fun I J K => bundleTransition_cocycle d r I J K)
1835	    (fun I => tautologicalQuotientComponent d r I)
1836	    (fun p => (tautologicalQuotientComponent_transpose d r p.1 p.2).mpr
1837	      (tautologicalQuotient_overlap d r p.1 p.2))

1839	/-! ## The functor of points and the universal property
1840	
1841	The Grassmannian functor sends a scheme `T` to the set of *equivalence classes* of
1842	rank-`d` quotients `q : O_T^r ↠ F` with `F` locally free of rank `d`. We encode a single
1843	such quotient as the structure `RankQuotient r d T`, the equivalence as `RankQuotient.Rel`
1844	(an isomorphism of the targets commuting with the quotient maps), and the functor's value
1845	at `T` as the quotient `Quotient (rqSetoid r d T)`. The pullback action `rqPullback`
1846	together with `pullbackFreeIso`/`pullback_isLocallyFreeOfRank` (and the fact that the
1847	pullback functor preserves epimorphisms, being a left adjoint) realise functoriality.
1848	
1849	Because a sheaf of modules `F : T.Modules` is a large object, this quotient lives in
1850	`Type 1` (not `Type 0` as the original scaffold signature stated); the corrected universe
1851	is the only change to the pinned signature, and it is forced — the substantive content is
1852	unchanged. -/
1853	
1854	/-- A **rank-`d` quotient of `O_T^r`** on a scheme `T`: a sheaf of modules `F` on `T`,
1855	locally free of rank `d`, together with an epimorphism `q : O_T^r ↠ F`. This is the
1856	unbundled datum whose equivalence classes form the value of the Grassmannian functor. -/
1857	structure RankQuotient (r d : ℕ) (T : Scheme.{0}) where
1858	  /-- The quotient sheaf. -/
1859	  F : T.Modules
1860	  /-- The quotient map out of the trivial rank-`r` bundle. -/
1861	  q : SheafOfModules.free (R := T.ringCatSheaf) (Fin r) ⟶ F
1862	  /-- The quotient map is an epimorphism (surjective). -/
1863	  epi : Epi q
1864	  /-- The quotient sheaf is locally free of rank `d`. -/
1865	  locFree : SheafOfModules.IsLocallyFreeOfRank F d
1866	
1867	/-- Two rank-`d` quotients are **equivalent** when there is an isomorphism of the targets
1868	commuting with the quotient maps (equivalently, when the kernels of the quotient maps
1869	coincide). -/
1870	def RankQuotient.Rel {r d : ℕ} {T : Scheme.{0}} (x y : RankQuotient r d T) : Prop :=
1871	  ∃ f : x.F ≅ y.F, x.q ≫ f.hom = y.q
1872	
1873	lemma RankQuotient.rel_refl {r d : ℕ} {T : Scheme.{0}} (x : RankQuotient r d T) : x.Rel x :=
1874	  ⟨Iso.refl _, Category.comp_id _⟩
1875	
1876	lemma RankQuotient.rel_symm {r d : ℕ} {T : Scheme.{0}} {x y : RankQuotient r d T}
1877	    (h : x.Rel y) : y.Rel x := by
1878	  obtain ⟨f, hf⟩ := h
1879	  exact ⟨f.symm, by rw [Iso.symm_hom, Iso.comp_inv_eq]; exact hf.symm⟩
1880	
1881	lemma RankQuotient.rel_trans {r d : ℕ} {T : Scheme.{0}} {x y z : RankQuotient r d T}
1882	    (h1 : x.Rel y) (h2 : y.Rel z) : x.Rel z := by
1883	  obtain ⟨f, hf⟩ := h1; obtain ⟨g, hg⟩ := h2
1884	  -- term-mode (the `T.Modules` def-diamond blocks positional category `rw`)
1885	  exact ⟨f ≪≫ g,
1886	    (congrArg (x.q ≫ ·) (Iso.trans_hom f g)).trans <|
1887	      (Category.assoc x.q f.hom g.hom).symm.trans <|
1888	        (congrArg (· ≫ g.hom) hf).trans hg⟩
1889	
1890	/-- The equivalence-of-quotients setoid on `RankQuotient r d T`. -/
1891	instance rqSetoid (r d : ℕ) (T : Scheme.{0}) : Setoid (RankQuotient r d T) where
1892	  r := RankQuotient.Rel
1893	  iseqv := ⟨RankQuotient.rel_refl, RankQuotient.rel_symm, RankQuotient.rel_trans⟩
1894	
1895	/-- The **pullback action** on a rank-`d` quotient: pull the target sheaf and quotient map
1896	back along `ψ`, re-presenting the source as the trivial bundle via `pullbackFreeIso`. The
1897	result is again an epimorphism (pullback preserves epis) onto a rank-`d` locally free sheaf
1898	(`pullback_isLocallyFreeOfRank`). -/
1899	noncomputable def rqPullback {r d : ℕ} {T' T : Scheme.{0}} (ψ : T' ⟶ T)
1900	    (x : RankQuotient r d T) : RankQuotient r d T' where
1901	  F := (Scheme.Modules.pullback ψ).obj x.F
1902	  q := (Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso ψ (Fin r)).inv ≫ (Scheme.Modules.pullback ψ).map x.q
1903	  epi :=
1904	    -- fully explicit: the def-diamond on `T.Modules` blocks `Epi`-instance search, so
1905	    -- `x.epi` is threaded through `map_epi`/`epi_comp` by hand
1906	    @CategoryTheory.epi_comp _ _ _ _ _
1907	      (Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso ψ (Fin r)).inv inferInstance
1908	      ((Scheme.Modules.pullback ψ).map x.q)
1909	      (@CategoryTheory.Functor.map_epi _ _ _ _ (Scheme.Modules.pullback ψ) inferInstance _ _
1910	        x.q x.epi)
1911	  locFree := Scheme.Modules.pullback_isLocallyFreeOfRank ψ x.locFree
1912	
1913	/-- The pullback action respects the equivalence relation, hence descends to quotients. -/
1914	lemma rqPullback_rel {r d : ℕ} {T' T : Scheme.{0}} (ψ : T' ⟶ T)
1915	    {x y : RankQuotient r d T} (h : x.Rel y) :
1916	    (rqPullback ψ x).Rel (rqPullback ψ y) := by
1917	  obtain ⟨f, hf⟩ := h
1918	  refine ⟨(Scheme.Modules.pullback ψ).mapIso f, ?_⟩
1919	  change ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso ψ (Fin r)).inv ≫ (Scheme.Modules.pullback ψ).map x.q) ≫
1920	      (Scheme.Modules.pullback ψ).map f.hom
1921	    = (Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso ψ (Fin r)).inv ≫ (Scheme.Modules.pullback ψ).map y.q
1922	  rw [Category.assoc, ← (Scheme.Modules.pullback ψ).map_comp]
1923	  exact congrArg
1924	    (fun m => (Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso ψ (Fin r)).inv ≫ (Scheme.Modules.pullback ψ).map m) hf
1925	
1926	end AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian

1930	/-- The **Grassmannian functor** `Grass(r,d)` (`def:grassmannian_functor`): the
1931	contravariant functor from schemes to sets sending `T` to the set of equivalence classes
1932	of rank-`d` locally free quotients `q : O_T^r ↠ F`, acting on morphisms by pullback.
1933	
1934	The object and morphism assignments are complete; the functoriality laws (`map_id`,
1935	`map_comp`) are discharged — via the naturality of the pseudofunctor comparison isomorphisms
1936	`pullbackId`/`pullbackComp` of `Scheme.Modules.pullback` — through the free-sheaf coherences
1937	`pullbackFreeIso_id`/`pullbackFreeIso_comp`, which reduce by coproduct extensionality to the
1938	unit-level coherences `pullbackObjUnitToUnit_id`/`pullbackObjUnitToUnit_comp`. Fully proved. -/
1939	noncomputable def functor (d r : ℕ) : Scheme.{0}ᵒᵖ ⥤ Type 1 where
1940	  obj T := Quotient (rqSetoid r d T.unop)
1941	  map {X Y} g := TypeCat.ofHom (Quotient.map (rqPullback (r := r) (d := d) g.unop)
1942	    (fun _ _ h => rqPullback_rel g.unop h))
1943	  map_id X := by
1944	    -- reduce to the equivalence relation on representatives
1945	    ext z
1946	    induction z using Quotient.ind with
1947	    | _ x =>
1948	      change Quotient.mk _ (rqPullback (𝟙 X).unop x) = Quotient.mk _ x
1949	      -- the canonical iso `(𝟙)^* x.F ≅ x.F` is `pullbackId`; the quotient-map equation it
1950	      -- must satisfy reduces, by naturality of `pullbackId.hom`, to the free coherence
1951	      -- `pullbackFreeIso (𝟙) = (pullbackId).app (free _)`, hence to the unit-level identity
1952	      -- `pullbackObjUnitToUnit (𝟙) = (pullbackId).app unit`. That coherence between
1953	      -- `SheafOfModules.pullbackObjFreeIso` and Mathlib's `pullbackId` is the open obstacle.
1954	      refine Quotient.sound ⟨(Scheme.Modules.pullbackId X.unop).app x.F, ?_⟩
1955	      -- unfold `(rqPullback (𝟙) x).q` and `(pullbackId.app x.F).hom` (defeq)
1956	      change ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso (𝟙 X.unop) (Fin r)).inv ≫
1957	          (Scheme.Modules.pullback (𝟙 X.unop)).map x.q) ≫
1958	          (Scheme.Modules.pullbackId X.unop).hom.app x.F = x.q
1959	      rw [Category.assoc, (Scheme.Modules.pullbackId X.unop).hom.naturality x.q,
1960	        ← Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso_id]
1961	      -- `(𝟭).map x.q = x.q` is only defeq, so close by term (rw can't see through it)
1962	      exact Iso.inv_hom_id_assoc _ _
1963	  map_comp {X Y Z} f g := by
1964	    ext z
1965	    induction z using Quotient.ind with
1966	    | _ x =>
1967	      change Quotient.mk _ (rqPullback (f ≫ g).unop x)
1968	        = Quotient.mk _ (rqPullback g.unop (rqPullback f.unop x))
1969	      -- the canonical iso `(g.unop ≫ f.unop)^* x.F ≅ g.unop^*(f.unop^* x.F)` is `pullbackComp`;
1970	      -- the quotient-map equation reduces, by naturality, to the composite free coherence
1971	      -- relating `pullbackFreeIso (g.unop ≫ f.unop)` to `pullbackFreeIso g.unop`/`f.unop`
1972	      -- through `pullbackComp` — the composite analogue of the `map_id` obstacle.
1973	      refine Quotient.sound ⟨((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp g.unop f.unop).app x.F).symm, ?_⟩
1974	      -- unfold `(rqPullback (g∘f) x).q` and `(pullbackComp.app x.F).symm.hom` (defeq), writing
1975	      -- the composite as `g.unop ≫ f.unop` so the `pullbackComp` naturality matches syntactically
1976	      change ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso (g.unop ≫ f.unop) (Fin r)).inv ≫
1977	          (Scheme.Modules.pullback (g.unop ≫ f.unop)).map x.q) ≫
1978	          (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp g.unop f.unop).inv.app x.F
1979	        = (rqPullback g.unop (rqPullback f.unop x)).q
1980	      -- expose the `pullbackComp.inv` naturality square (mirrors the `map_id` reduction)
1981	      rw [Category.assoc, (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp g.unop f.unop).inv.naturality x.q]
1982	      -- the composite free coherence (`pullbackFreeIso_comp`) in inverse form: invert both
1983	      -- sides of the iso equation `pullbackComp.hom.app free ≫ pfba.hom = (pullback g).map pfa.hom
1984	      -- ≫ pfb.hom`.
1985	      have hstar : (Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso (g.unop ≫ f.unop) (Fin r)).inv ≫
1986	            (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp g.unop f.unop).inv.app (SheafOfModules.free (Fin r))
1987	          = (Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso g.unop (Fin r)).inv ≫
1988	            (Scheme.Modules.pullback g.unop).map
1989	              (Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso f.unop (Fin r)).inv := by
1990	        have hH := Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso_comp f.unop g.unop (Fin r)
1991	        rw [← cancel_epi ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp g.unop f.unop).hom.app
1992	          (SheafOfModules.free (Fin r)) ≫
1993	          (Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso (g.unop ≫ f.unop) (Fin r)).hom)]
1994	        trans (𝟙 _)
1995	        · rw [Category.assoc, Iso.hom_inv_id_assoc]
1996	          exact (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp g.unop f.unop).hom_inv_id_app _
1997	        · rw [hH]; simp
1998	      -- whisker `hstar` by `≫ (pullback f ⋙ pullback g).map x.q` and refold the RHS via
1999	      -- `map_comp` into `(rqPullback g (rqPullback f x)).q`.
2000	      exact (Category.assoc _ _ _).symm.trans
2001	        ((congrArg (· ≫ (Scheme.Modules.pullback f.unop ⋙
2002	              Scheme.Modules.pullback g.unop).map x.q) hstar).trans
2003	          ((Category.assoc _ _ _).trans
2004	            (congrArg ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso g.unop (Fin r)).inv ≫ ·)
2005	              ((Scheme.Modules.pullback g.unop).map_comp
2006	                (Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso f.unop (Fin r)).inv
2007	                ((Scheme.Modules.pullback f.unop).map x.q)).symm)))
2008	
2009	/-- **Chart restriction of the universal quotient sheaf**: over the `I`-th chart, the
2010	universal bundle `U` restricts to the free rank-`d` sheaf — the instantiation of the
2011	descent restriction isomorphism `Scheme.Modules.glueRestrictionIso` at the Grassmannian
2012	glue data. (Its underlying morphism is the adjoint transpose of the `I`-th
2013	descent-equalizer projection; iso-ness rides on
2014	`Scheme.Modules.isIso_glueRestrictionHom`.) -/
2015	noncomputable def universalQuotient_restrictionIso (d r : ℕ) (I : (theGlueData d r).J) :
2016	    (Scheme.Modules.pullback ((theGlueData d r).ι I)).obj (universalQuotient d r)
2017	      ≅ SheafOfModules.free (R := ((theGlueData d r).U I).ringCatSheaf) (Fin d) :=
2018	  Scheme.Modules.glueRestrictionIso (theGlueData d r)
2019	    (fun I => SheafOfModules.free (R := ((theGlueData d r).U I).ringCatSheaf) (Fin d))
2020	    (bundleTransitionData d r)
2021	    (fun I => bundleTransition_self d r I.1 I.2)
2022	    (fun I J K => bundleTransition_cocycle d r I J K) I
2023	
2024	/-- **The universal quotient sheaf is locally free of rank `d`**
2025	(`thm:grassmannian_universal_property`, first ingredient): the chart images
2026	`{ι_I(U^I)}` cover the glued scheme, and on each member the restriction of
2027	`universalQuotient` is identified with `O^d` by transporting the descent restriction
2028	isomorphism `universalQuotient_restrictionIso` along the factorization
2029	`ι_I = isoOpensRange.hom ≫ opensRange.ι`. -/
2030	theorem universalQuotient_isLocallyFreeOfRank (d r : ℕ) :
2031	    SheafOfModules.IsLocallyFreeOfRank (universalQuotient d r) d := by
2032	  refine ⟨(theGlueData d r).J, fun I => ((theGlueData d r).ι I).opensRange, ?_, fun I => ?_⟩
2033	  · rw [eq_top_iff]
2034	    intro x _
2035	    obtain ⟨I, y, rfl⟩ := (theGlueData d r).ι_jointly_surjective x
2036	    exact TopologicalSpace.Opens.mem_iSup.mpr ⟨I, y, rfl⟩
2037	  · -- transport the chart restriction iso along `ι_I = isoOpensRange.hom ≫ opensRange.ι`,
2038	    -- inverting the chart-parametrization iso via the pullback pseudofunctor
2039	    refine ⟨?_⟩
2040	    letI ι := (theGlueData d r).ι I
2041	    letI e := ι.isoOpensRange
2042	    exact (Scheme.Modules.pullbackId _).symm.app _ ≪≫
2043	      (Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr (Iso.inv_hom_id e).symm).app _ ≪≫
2044	      ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp e.inv e.hom).app _).symm ≪≫
2045	      (Scheme.Modules.pullback e.inv).mapIso
2046	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp e.hom ι.opensRange.ι).app (universalQuotient d r) ≪≫
2047	          (Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr (ι.isoOpensRange_hom_ι)).app (universalQuotient d r) ≪≫
2048	          universalQuotient_restrictionIso d r I) ≪≫
2049	      Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso e.inv (Fin d) ≪≫
2050	      SheafOfModules.freeFunctor.mapIso (Equiv.ulift.symm.toIso)
2051	
2052	/-- **The tautological quotient is an epimorphism**
2053	(`thm:grassmannian_universal_property`, second ingredient).
2054

2056	`glueLift_glueProj` along the chart immersion shows
2057	`ι_I^* (tautologicalQuotient) ≫ glueRestrictionHom I` equals (up to the free-pullback
2058	comparison `pullbackFreeIso`) the chart quotient `chartQuotientMap d r I.1 I.2`, which
2059	is a (split) epi (`chartQuotientMap_epi`). A family of morphisms out of the glued
2060	sheaf is jointly reflected by the chart restrictions (the separation half of the sheaf
2061	condition of the descent equalizer), so `q ≫ u = q ≫ v → u = v` follows once all chart
2062	restrictions of `q` are epi. The joint-reflection lemma is the missing ingredient —
2063	it shares its proof skeleton with `isIso_glueRestrictionHom` (mono-ness of
2064	`E ⟶ ∏ (ι_I)_* M_I`). -/
2065	theorem tautologicalQuotient_epi (d r : ℕ) : Epi (tautologicalQuotient d r) := by
2066	  sorry

2068	/-- **The tautological point of the Grassmannian**: the rank-`d` locally free quotient
2069	`u : O^r ↠ U` on `Gr(d,r)` itself, packaged as a `RankQuotient`. Pulling it back along
2070	`ψ : T ⟶ Gr(d,r)` realizes the forward direction of the universal property. -/
2071	noncomputable def tautologicalRankQuotient (d r : ℕ) : RankQuotient r d (scheme d r) where
2072	  F := universalQuotient d r
2073	  q := tautologicalQuotient d r
2074	  epi := tautologicalQuotient_epi d r
2075	  locFree := universalQuotient_isLocallyFreeOfRank d r

2077	/-! ### The Nitsure §1 inverse construction: chart loci, chart matrices, chart morphisms
2078	
2079	For a rank-`d` quotient `x = ⟨F, q⟩` on `T` and a size-`d` subset `I ⊆ Fin r`, the
2080	*chart composite* is `s_I ≫ q : O_T^d ⟶ F` (the `I`-indexed coordinate inclusion
2081	followed by the quotient map) and the *chart locus* `T_I ⊆ T` is the largest open on
2082	which it restricts to an isomorphism. The loci are open by construction (a supremum of
2083	opens), cover `T` (Nakayama at each point), and over `T_I` the quotient is presented by
2084	a `d × r` matrix of sections whose `I`-minor is the identity — its complementary
2085	entries determine a ring map `R^I ⟶ Γ(T_I, O)` and hence a morphism `T_I ⟶ U^I` by the
2086	Γ–Spec adjunction. These glue to the inverse `grPointOfRankQuotient` of the universal
2087	property. -/
2088	
2089	/-- The **iso-locus** of a morphism of sheaves of modules on a scheme: the supremum
2090	(union) of all opens `U` such that the pullback of `φ` to the open subscheme `U` is an
2091	isomorphism. This is the largest open on which `φ` is invertible. Project-local
2092	(Mathlib has no iso-locus for morphisms of sheaves of modules). -/
2093	def isoLocus {X : Scheme.{0}} {M N : X.Modules} (φ : M ⟶ N) : X.Opens :=
2094	  sSup {U : X.Opens | IsIso ((Scheme.Modules.pullback U.ι).map φ)}
2095	
2096	/-- Membership in the iso-locus: `t ∈ isoLocus φ` iff some open neighbourhood of `t`
2097	pulls `φ` back to an isomorphism. Project-local. -/
2098	lemma mem_isoLocus {X : Scheme.{0}} {M N : X.Modules} {φ : M ⟶ N} {t : X} :
2099	    t ∈ isoLocus φ
2100	      ↔ ∃ U : X.Opens, IsIso ((Scheme.Modules.pullback U.ι).map φ) ∧ t ∈ U := by
2101	  simp only [isoLocus, TopologicalSpace.Opens.mem_sSup, Set.mem_setOf_eq]
2102	
2103	/-- Restriction stability of pullback-invertibility: if `φ` pulls back to an
2104	isomorphism on `U` and `W ≤ U`, it pulls back to an isomorphism on `W` — transport
2105	along the pseudofunctor comparison `pullback W.ι ≅ pullback U.ι ⋙ pullback (homOfLE)`.
2106	Project-local. -/
2107	lemma isIso_pullback_map_of_le {X : Scheme.{0}} {M N : X.Modules} (φ : M ⟶ N)
2108	    {W U : X.Opens} (e : W ≤ U)
2109	    (hU : IsIso ((Scheme.Modules.pullback U.ι).map φ)) :
2110	    IsIso ((Scheme.Modules.pullback W.ι).map φ) := by
2111	  have h : IsIso ((Scheme.Modules.pullback U.ι ⋙
2112	      Scheme.Modules.pullback (X.homOfLE e)).map φ) := by
2113	    change IsIso ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (X.homOfLE e)).map
2114	      ((Scheme.Modules.pullback U.ι).map φ))
2115	    exact inferInstance
2116	  exact (NatIso.isIso_map_iff
2117	    (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp (X.homOfLE e) U.ι ≪≫
2118	      Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr (X.homOfLE_ι e)) φ).mp h
2119	
2120	/-- The **chart composite** `s_I ≫ q : O_T^d ⟶ F` (Nitsure §1): the `I`-indexed
2121	coordinate inclusion of free sheaves followed by the quotient map of the rank-`d`
2122	quotient `x`. Project-local. -/
2123	noncomputable def chartComposite {T : Scheme.{0}} {r d : ℕ} (x : RankQuotient r d T)
2124	    (I : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d) :
2125	    SheafOfModules.free (R := T.ringCatSheaf) (Fin d) ⟶ x.F :=
2126	  SheafOfModules.freeMap (fun j : Fin d => (I.orderIsoOfFin hI j : Fin r)) ≫ x.q
2127	
2128	/-- The **chart locus** `T_I ⊆ T` of a rank-`d` quotient (Nitsure §1): the largest open
2129	of `T` on which the chart composite `s_I ≫ q` restricts to an isomorphism. Project-local. -/
2130	noncomputable def chartLocus {T : Scheme.{0}} {r d : ℕ} (x : RankQuotient r d T)
2131	    (I : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d) : T.Opens :=
2132	  isoLocus (chartComposite x I hI)

2134	/-- **The chart loci cover `T`** (Nitsure §1, the Nakayama step): at each point `t`, the
2135	fibre `F ⊗ κ(t)` is a `d`-dimensional quotient of `κ(t)^r`, so some `d` of the standard
2136	basis vectors map to a basis; for that subset `I` the chart composite is an isomorphism
2137	near `t` (a surjective endomorphism argument / Nakayama), i.e. `t ∈ T_I`.
2138	
2139	PROOF ROUTE (scoped iter-067, not yet formalized): work in a trivialisation `V ∋ t`,

2141	`O_V`; epi-ness of `q` gives stalkwise surjectivity at `t`, so over the residue field
2142	some `d`-column minor of the presenting `d × r` matrix has nonzero determinant; shrink
2143	`V` to the basic open of that determinant, on which the matrix is invertible and hence
2144	the composite an isomorphism (`matrixToFreeIso`). -/
2145	theorem chartLocus_isOpenCover {T : Scheme.{0}} (d r : ℕ) (x : RankQuotient r d T) :
2146	    TopologicalSpace.IsOpenCover (fun I : (theGlueData d r).J => chartLocus x I.1 I.2) := by

2149	/-- **The chart composite is an isomorphism over the whole chart locus** — the local
2150	inverses glue (separation + gluing halves of the sheaf condition).

2158	theorem isIso_pullback_isoLocus_map {X : Scheme.{0}} {M N : X.Modules} (φ : M ⟶ N) :
2159	    IsIso ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (isoLocus φ).ι).map φ) := by

2162	/-- The `p`-th **projection of the free sheaf** `O_X^d ⟶ O_X`, through the
2163	biproduct/coproduct comparison. Companion of `SheafOfModules.ιFree`; project-local. -/
2164	noncomputable def projFree {X : Scheme.{0}} {d : ℕ} (p : Fin d) :
2165	    SheafOfModules.free (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (Fin d) ⟶ SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf :=
2166	  (biproduct.isoCoproduct (fun _ : Fin d => SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf)).inv ≫
2167	    biproduct.π (fun _ : Fin d => SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf) p
2168	
2169	/-- The global section of `O_X` carried by an endomorphism of the unit sheaf of modules:
2170	the value of the endomorphism at the unit section `1` over `⊤`. This inverts `scalarEnd`
2171	(`unitEndSection_scalarEnd`); project-local. -/
2172	noncomputable def unitEndSection {X : Scheme.{0}}

2303	    (e : SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf ⟶ SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf) :

2317	    (homOfLE (le_top : Y.unop ≤ ⊤)).op
